subject: Trump is coming to PA today
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CHRIS DELUZIO FOR CONGRESS
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120940797/587849282/1895761198?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzOTYwM2EyMi0yZGQ1LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=xR8GvWwQSMrr1e5uRMiUbKXQ5ye1mLKvtilFXWB9Bts=&emci=2d603a22-2dd5-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=39603a22-2dd5-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=13185894) **Donald Trump is coming to Pennsylvania TODAY to “highlight his efforts to tackle inflation”** _—a Day One promise he has failed to deliver._ Let’s be real: Trump’s not in Pennsylvania today to take on rising costs. He’s here to spin them. Families across PA are feeling the squeeze every single day, and Trump has made it worse: reckless trade wars, chaotic tariffs, and fat tax giveaways to huge corporations and the rich and powerful paid for with new debt and healthcare cuts. He knows he’s looking weak, so he’s here in PA—the most critical swing state—in some lame attempt to try and clean up his record of economic failure. [**Before he steps on that stage in PA, I want to show him something he can’t spin—this Western PA congressional seat is NOT up for grabs. Can you chip in today to help hold the line in PA-17 and flip the House blue?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120940798/587849283/320240526?refcode=email_120925_trump&amounts=5,15,25,50,100&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzOTYwM2EyMi0yZGQ1LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=xR8GvWwQSMrr1e5uRMiUbKXQ5ye1mLKvtilFXWB9Bts=) | | [Donate →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120940799/587849284/320240526?refcode=email_120925_trump&amounts=5,15,25,50,100&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzOTYwM2EyMi0yZGQ1LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=xR8GvWwQSMrr1e5uRMiUbKXQ5ye1mLKvtilFXWB9Bts=)
---
Trump pledged to bring down prices on day one in office. Instead, he tanked the economy with his reckless trade war, chaotic tariffs, and cuts to new energy projects.
Pennsylvanians are fed up, and who can blame us? Trump is driving up costs while shoving the American Dream further out of reach—further rigging the economy for the rich and powerful.
**And now he’s marching into Pennsylvania like we’re just going to forget any of that? Not a chance.**
Remember—Republicans have full control of our government, and life is even more of a ripoff than when they took power. Yet they want to hold onto that power in Washington. It’s up to us to deliver a defeat to this failing agenda.
[**PA-17 is one of the biggest roadblocks to any hope Republicans and Trump have to hold onto their House majority. clean_body: Donald Trump is coming to Pennsylvania TODAY to “highlight his efforts to tackle inflation” —a Day One promise he has failed to deliver. Let’s be real: Trump’s not in Pennsylvania today to take on rising costs. He’s here to spin them. Families across PA are feeling the squeeze every single day, and Trump has made it worse: reckless trade wars, chaotic tariffs, and fat tax giveaways to huge corporations and the rich and powerful paid for with new debt and healthcare cuts. He knows he’s looking weak, so he’s here in PA—the most critical swing state—in some lame attempt to try and clean up his record of economic failure. = | | = Trump pledged to bring down prices on day one in office. Instead, he tanked the economy with his reckless trade war, chaotic tariffs, and cuts to new energy projects. Pennsylvanians are fed up, and who can blame us? Trump is driving up costs while shoving the American Dream further out of reach—further rigging the economy for the rich and powerful. And now he’s marching into Pennsylvania like we’re just going to forget any of that? Not a chance. Remember—Republicans have full control of our government, and life is even more of a ripoff than when they took power. Yet they want to hold onto that power in Washington. clean_body: grassroots support is critical| | | Patriot, it’s been a big year. In the Senate, we’ve delivered President Trump’s agenda, cut your taxes, and secured our border. I’ve officially filed to run for re-election and I’m proud to be endorsed by President Trump, Governor Sarah Sanders, and conservative leaders across the country. We’re in for a fight come 2026. Democrats have their eyes on Arkansas. They’re running the same playbook they always do: Pick a far-left fraud and send their campaign millions to dupe voters about their record. There’s no path to keeping the Senate Majority without keeping Arkansas red. So that’s why it’s so important we earn as many grassroots endorsements as we can, before the attack ads even get started. Thank you for standing with me before in defense of our conservative values and the fight to make America strong again. In service, Tom Cotton | | Senator Cotton was honorably discharged from the U.S. Army. Images do not imply endorsement by the Department of Defense or any Service Branch. PAID FOR BY COTTON FOR SENATE.

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. name: Wes Moore
subject: You, me, and the Ravens (or O’s!) — what do you say?
body:
I am looking for a special guest to join me at an upcoming game in Baltimore. Are you interested?
# [](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/8?t=1&akid=1035%2E1086043%2EACq2Ck)
Peter,
Nothing... and I mean _nothing..._ beats the energy of game day in Maryland.
The roar of the crowd when Lamar Jackson scrambles for 12 yards. That crack of the bat when Gunnar Henderson cranks a homer at the historic Camden Yards. Maryland knows how to show up and show out for our teams.
And I’ve gotten to attend quite a few games here in Baltimore. Consider it one of the perks of being governor! But I’d love some company next time, so we’re going to try something fun. **I am looking for a special guest to join me at a game in Baltimore — and I want it to be you, Peter!**
**[Chip in any amount to enter and help power our campaign. Donate $5 or whatever you can right now to support our re-election, and you’ll be automatically entered for a chance to win a ticket to join me at a Ravens or Orioles game.](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-game-with-governor-contest.MC8mZH&refcode=em-20251209-1&t=2&akid=1035%2E1086043%2EACq2Ck)**
[](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-game-with-governor-contest.MC8mZH&refcode=em-20251209-1&t=3&akid=1035%2E1086043%2EACq2Ck)
Here is the deal: Our team is going to pick one winner to come join me at a Ravens or Orioles game. We’re talking:
* **Great view and good company:** One ticket to join me during the game.
* **Food and drinks:** Hot dogs, peanuts, and drinks of your choice on us.
* **A souvenir:** I’ll sign your ticket so you have something to remember the day.

This campaign is about bringing people together. I can think of no better way to celebrate that than over a beer and a game in Baltimore.
**[Are you in? Donate $5 or whatever you can today. You’ll be automatically entered to win and join me at an upcoming Ravens or Orioles game.](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-game-with-governor-contest.MC8mZH&refcode=em-20251209-1&t=4&akid=1035%2E1086043%2EACq2Ck)**
**[Enter to Win ››](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-game-with-governor-contest.MC8mZH&amount=5&refcode=em-20251209-1&t=5&akid=1035%2E1086043%2EACq2Ck)**
This is easily my new favorite way to raise money for a good cause, win for all of Maryland, and have fun while we’re at it. Can’t wait to see who’ll join me!
Elevate,
Wes
[](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-game-with-governor-contest.MC8mZH&refcode=em-20251209-1&t=6&akid=1035%2E1086043%2EACq2Ck)

**[Enter to Win ››](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-game-with-governor-contest.MC8mZH&amount=5&refcode=em-20251209-1&t=7&akid=1035%2E1086043%2EACq2Ck)**
_No purchase, payment, or contribution necessary to enter to win. Void where prohibited. Entries must be received by 11:59 p.m. on September 1, 2026. You may enter by making a donation[here.](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/855?t=8&akid=1035%2E1086043%2EACq2Ck) Whole or partial donations of up to $5 will receive one entry. A maximum of $50 of an individual’s contributions may be counted towards entries. Alternatively, you can enter once without making a donation [here.](https://act.wesmoore.com/signup/wes-game-with-governor-contest/?t=9&akid=1035%2E1086043%2EACq2Ck) One (1) winner will receive 1 ticket to accompany Governor Moore to an upcoming Maryland-based professional sports game of the campaign’s choosing (Ravens/NFL or Orioles/MLB), as well as free food and drink at that game. Ticket will be signed by Governor Moore at the event. Retail value of the prize does not exceed $750. Sponsor will not pay for travel, hotel stay, or any other expenses related to the Contest for a winner. Odds of winning depend on number of eligible entries received. Promotion open to U.S. citizens, or lawful permanent U.S. residents who are legal residents of the 50 United States and District of Columbia and 18 or older (or of majority under applicable law), and are permissible contributors under Maryland law. Promotion subject to Official Rules and additional restrictions on eligibility here. Sponsor: Wes Moore for Maryland, PO Box 50123 Baltimore, MD 21211._

* * *
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.wesmoore.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=10&akid=1035%2E1086043%2EACq2Ck)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.wesmoore.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.wesmoore.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=11&akid=1035%2E1086043%2EACq2Ck)

Email us: [info@wesmoore.com](mailto:info@wesmoore.com)
BY AUTHORITY: WES MOORE FOR MARYLAND, MARY TYDINGS, TREASURER

name: James Carville
subject: Republicans might win in New Hampshire.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chris Pappas for Senate
body:
If we lose New Hampshire, we lose any shot at flipping the Senate. Period.
Folks, it’s James Carville.
If I had any hair, I’d be ripping it out right now!!
Democrats’ chances of winning the New Hampshire Senate race have _officially been downgraded.**That means Republicans are in spitting distance of flipping this seat and cutting off ANY chance Democrats have of retaking control of the Senate next year._**
**[Before you read another word, chip in $5 to help Chris Pappas hold the line in New Hampshire.](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/221?c0=em_ms_cp_20251209_1_carville.L1LzUn&refcode=em20251209-1&t=1&akid=3931%2E582294%2E1ft41q)**
**[Rush $5 to Chris Pappas Right Now!](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251209_1_carville.BORGTN&amount=5&refcode=em20251209-1&t=2&akid=3931%2E582294%2E1ft41q)**
Let me be crystal clear. If Democrats blow it in New Hampshire, we have NO path to taking back the Senate. Not a snowball’s chance in hell.
Any guess who the GOP recruited into the race? John Sununu. Yes, that same Sununu family. John’s got the famous last name, he’s got Wall Street’s money, and he’s got the full force of the national Republican establishment behind him.
Chris Pappas is the only thing standing in their way. Chris isn’t some lightweight. He’s won one of the toughest Congressional districts in the country four times running. He knows how to fight and win.
**_But — with Sununu in the race — if we don’t rally around Chris Pappas right now, we’re going to get steamrolled!_**
So here’s what I need from you: **[Chip in $5, or more if you can, to help Chris Pappas fight back and hold this seat for the Democrats.](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/221?c0=em_ms_cp_20251209_1_carville.L1LzUn&refcode=em20251209-1&t=3&akid=3931%2E582294%2E1ft41q)**
**[Rush $5 to Chris Pappas Right Now!](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251209_1_carville.BORGTN&amount=5&refcode=em20251209-1&t=4&akid=3931%2E582294%2E1ft41q)**
_This is the GOP’s dream scenario:_ an open seat in a swing state, a big-name Republican, and a whole lot of dark money to burn. I know from my years of campaigning that this is exactly what they were hoping for.
**It’s time to get off our butts and fight like hell for Chris.** Let’s show these Republican bigshots that they can’t just waltz in and buy New Hampshire. Not on our watch.
**[Give what you can right now.](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/221?c0=em_ms_cp_20251209_1_carville.L1LzUn&refcode=em20251209-1&t=5&akid=3931%2E582294%2E1ft41q)** Let’s win this damn thing and flip the Senate.
— James Carville
[](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/221?c0=em_ms_cp_20251209_1_carville.L1LzUn&refcode=em20251209-1&t=6&akid=3931%2E582294%2E1ft41q)

clean_body: If we lose New Hampshire, we lose any shot at flipping the Senate. Period. Folks, it’s James Carville. If I had any hair, I’d be ripping it out right now!! Democrats’ chances of winning the New Hampshire Senate race have officially been downgraded.That means Republicans are in spitting distance of flipping this seat and cutting off ANY chance Democrats have of retaking control of the Senate next year. Let me be crystal clear. If Democrats blow it in New Hampshire, we have NO path to taking back the Senate. Not a snowball’s chance in hell. Any guess who the GOP recruited into the race? John Sununu. Yes, that same Sununu family. John’s got the famous last name, he’s got Wall Street’s money, and he’s got the full force of the national Republican establishment behind him. Chris Pappas is the only thing standing in their way. Chris isn’t some lightweight. He’s won one of the toughest Congressional districts in the country four times running. He knows how to fight and win. But — with Sununu in the race — if we don’t rally around Chris Pappas right now, we’re going to get steamrolled! So here’s what I need from you: This is the GOP’s dream scenario: an open seat in a swing state, a big-name Republican, and a whole lot of dark money to burn. I know from my years of campaigning that this is exactly what they were hoping for. It’s time to get off our butts and fight like hell for Chris. Let’s show these Republican bigshots that they can’t just waltz in and buy New Hampshire. Not on our watch. Let’s win this damn thing and flip the Senate. — James Carville

_Every_ Coloradan will feel the effects of this win. And so will Americans across the country.
In Colorado, Planned Parenthood provides a wide range of services, from abortion care to wellness and preventative care to STI testing to mental health services. Stripping Medicaid funding from Planned Parenthood threatened to prevent Coloradans from accessing **an important source of medical care. We stopped that from happening.**
**Colorado is an island of abortion care access for the western United States.** Six out of our seven neighboring states have more restrictive abortion laws than we do, which makes it even more crucial for Coloradans to be able to access care where they live.
The data is clear: the risk of dying during pregnancy is 2X higher in states with abortion bans. Being denied this choice can force women into worse financial, health, and family outcomes. As your governor, I will always defend Coloradans’ right to make their own reproductive health care decisions.
**[I’ll never appease President Trump’s lawless intimidation or his destructive politics. I’m here in Colorado, fighting for your reproductive freedom. If that makes your choice clear, chip in today.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120941288/587850629/-198793374?refcode=em_WEI_FR_PlannedParenthood_120925&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkODcwZTMyYi0yZmQ1LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3hdnOpnItGSIk-OKxtlqnS7GUVTXlNE_cnGFLxy6D9M=)**

body:
Together, we’re going to flip Texas blue

| |

_Seth Moulton here. Jasmine Crockett is running for Senate in Texas, so I wanted to pass along this message and ask that you **[split a contribution](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120941440/587851072/32574383?refcode=251209_jc_sm_split_l1&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MGQ4ZDQ1MS0yZmQ1LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=P_qWKQOoK8lKbEkYg5P0ahEBemjG_7HvTNeYui5eiNA=)** between my campaign and hers._ [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120941441/587851073/1745195860?refcode=251209_jc_sm_split_img&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MGQ4ZDQ1MS0yZmQ1LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=P_qWKQOoK8lKbEkYg5P0ahEBemjG_7HvTNeYui5eiNA=)

Y’all, it’s Jasmine. I’m reaching out with big news, and I wanted you to hear it from me first: **I’m officially running for the United States Senate here in Texas.** Let me be clear: With our current wannabe dictator in the White House, **I’m not launching this campaign to play nice. I’m doing it to fight — because Texas deserves someone who doesn’t back down when the bullies come for her. _It deserves someone who fights back._** But to win this race, we’ll have to take on Donald Trump, the Texas Republican political machine, and all of their shady special interest donors. _And to be blunt, that won’t come cheap._

[**That’s why I’m asking you to become one of my founding donors as we launch this historic grassroots campaign. I’m counting on your support now more than ever before so that we can hit the ground running and finally flip Texas blue. Will you answer the call by splitting a donation between my campaign and Seth Moulton?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120941442/587851074/1745195860?refcode=251209_jc_sm_split_l2&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MGQ4ZDQ1MS0yZmQ1LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=P_qWKQOoK8lKbEkYg5P0ahEBemjG_7HvTNeYui5eiNA=) | [Give $25 >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120941449/587851081/32574383?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=251209_jc_sm_split&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MGQ4ZDQ1MS0yZmQ1LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=P_qWKQOoK8lKbEkYg5P0ahEBemjG_7HvTNeYui5eiNA=) | [Give $50 >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120941450/587851082/32574383?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=251209_jc_sm_split&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MGQ4ZDQ1MS0yZmQ1LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=P_qWKQOoK8lKbEkYg5P0ahEBemjG_7HvTNeYui5eiNA=)

Let me be clear: **When there’s a fight worth having, I don’t flinch. I don’t shrink. I don’t back down.**
Just ask the communities I’ve fought for as a civil-rights attorney, the defendants I’ve represented as a public defender, and the people of Dallas that I serve in Congress.**I’m a lifelong fighter for justice.**
So Donald Trump can run his mouth all he wants. All his attacks show is that he’s terrified of what I represent — and what I’m bringing to the Senate when we win this race: **truth, accountability, and a future where power answers to the people.**

**[If you believe that insults won’t silence us — if you believe in a Texas that stands for justice, equity, and real representation — I need you. We’re launching a campaign to change the game. But real campaigns need real support. That’s why I’d be so grateful if you could pitch in $10, $25, or more to help us hit the ground running. Jasmine Crockett is running for Senate in Texas, so I wanted to pass along this message and ask that you = between my campaign and hers. = Y’all, it’s Jasmine. I’m reaching out with big news, and I wanted you to hear it from me first: I’m officially running for the United States Senate here in Texas. Let me be clear: With our current wannabe dictator in the White House, I’m not launching this campaign to play nice. I’m doing it to fight — because Texas deserves someone who doesn’t back down when the bullies come for her. It deserves someone who fights back. But to win this race, we’ll have to take on Donald Trump, the Texas Republican political machine, and all of their shady special interest donors. And to be blunt, that won’t come cheap. = | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately. Let me be clear: When there’s a fight worth having, I don’t flinch. I don’t shrink. I don’t back down. Just ask the communities I’ve fought for as a civil-rights attorney, the defendants I’ve represented as a public defender, and the people of Dallas that I serve in Congress.I’m a lifelong fighter for justice. So Donald Trump can run his mouth all he wants. All his attacks show is that he’s terrified of what I represent — and what I’m bringing to the Senate when we win this race: truth, accountability, and a future where power answers to the people. = = | = ---|--- = | = = | = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately. subject: China is taking over the USA one farm at a time.

body:
It’s time to draw a line in the sand. Please read:

Peter,
Whether you want to admit it or not, the Chinese Communist Party is quietly taking over America one farm at a time.
China already owns land in **27 states** , including properties alarmingly close to U.S. military bases. **
* **Our ****farmers are being undercut.**
* **Our food supply is at risk.**
* **The CCP is expanding its footprint on American soil.**

** We’re not just facing a serious economic threat. This is a full-blown national security crisis.
**It’s time to draw the line.**
President Trump’s Cabinet, including Treasury Secretary Bessent, is working overtime to rein in the abuse China has inflicted on the United States for far too long. But that’s not enough.
We need grassroots support to stop this takeover. That means citizens like YOU stepping up and demanding real action to thwart China’s takeover efforts.
[Peter, we’re begging you to add your name RIGHT NOW and support a federal ban on China buying U.S. farmland:](https://trk.emails.troydowning._cmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmYW1vdW50PTM1JmV4X3RpZD0yMDI1MTIwOV9TUy1LVi4xMTAxMTdfdDE3Njk4NzgtMjg5OSZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249MjAyNTEyMDlfU1MtS1YuMTEwMTE3X3QxNzY5ODc4LTI4OTkifQ/NA6oVlawTN_2j1LPCRCM_Q) [](https://trk.emails.troydowning._cmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmYW1vdW50PTM1JmV4X3RpZD0yMDI1MTIwOV9TUy1LVi4xMTAxMTdfdDE3Njk4NzgtMjg5OSZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249MjAyNTEyMDlfU1MtS1YuMTEwMTE3X3QxNzY5ODc4LTI4OTkifQ/kZzmKAYZNbZjJVU93kg1vQ) [ BAN CHINA FROM BUYING OUR LAND

# TUESDAY, December 9, 2025

subject: Well-funded organizations are doubling down

body:
Map manipulators are trying to redraw their way into power and steal our power.
|
---

It’s working, Peter: Map manipulators are starting to feel real backlash from Americans who are sick and tired of partisan power grabs.
Over the past few months, people like you have stepped up at unprecedented levels to fight back against an existential threat to our representative democracy.
**But instead of backing down in the face of this backlash, they’re doubling down.**
Already, anti-democracy organizations are ramping up their plans to rig electoral maps in the months ahead — everywhere from Florida to New Hampshire. They’re spending big in states like Indiana to pressure state lawmakers to back down and follow marching orders from Washington, D.C., to redraw their congressional map.
And they’re watching the Supreme Court closely to see if they’ll be handed an opportunity to pursue a full-court press of new gerrymanders across the country if the Voting Rights Act is weakened.
[**We can ultimately win this fight, but only if we can keep up the pressure and have the resources to keep pace with the well-funded groups eager to manipulate maps in the pursuit of power. Donate to support All On The Line’s work organizing and advocating for fairness in 2026 and beyond.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120942530/587855219/798345718?refcode=don_aotl_tier1_email_DoublingDown_email_20251209-AOTL-Tier1-DoublingDown_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20251209-AOTL-Tier1-DoublingDown_&utm_campaign=DoublingDown&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImIyMjRkMzQ2LTMxZDUtZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=mkzqzic6v6gQ3w-LeVMry34-xru0K679tjePjl_nWMI=)

Folks, what's happening is so clear: Trump and his MAGA cronies are fundamentally transforming our Department of Justice and **turning it into a tool for undermining voting rights, not protecting them.** Under his watch, the DOJ and its Civil Rights Division -- _once lauded as the "crown jewel" of the Justice Department_ \-- has **abandoned its nonpartisan mission of defending the freedom to vote.** Instead, it’s targeting election officials, amplifying debunked voter fraud claims, and trying to intimidate states into giving election data. Voter Action Project was founded in 2020 to fight for the use of mail-in ballots under a hostile Trump administration during a global pandemic. **We're still fighting right now, working every day to protect our fundamental right to vote** under yet another vicious administration that's looking to trample all over American citizens and our ability to make our voice heard through our elections. **[In the face of escalating attacks on our right to vote, increasing amounts of voter disinformation, and voter intimidation tactics on the rise – will you stand with us and chip in $25 today to help us defend voting rights?>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120942669/587855767/1498593520?refcode=120925_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg5MjE1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImY3YjU3M2FmLTMxZDUtZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=-bjXyYF5cAl9c3g5L7OoWaGdGXE4WsCe_682_wNcI3I=)**

clean_body: CNN: Inside Trump’s gutting of the Justice Department unit that enforces voting laws| | | | | = Folks, what's happening is so clear: Trump and his MAGA cronies are fundamentally transforming our Department of Justice and turning it into a tool for undermining voting rights, not protecting them. Under his watch, the DOJ and its Civil Rights Division -- once lauded as the "crown jewel" of the Justice Department \-- has abandoned its nonpartisan mission of defending the freedom to vote. Instead, it’s targeting election officials, amplifying debunked voter fraud claims, and trying to intimidate states into giving election data. Voter Action Project was founded in 2020 to fight for the use of mail-in ballots under a hostile Trump administration during a global pandemic. We're still fighting right now, working every day to protect our fundamental right to vote under yet another vicious administration that's looking to trample all over American citizens and our ability to make our voice heard through our elections. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = | = | = | | = | = | = | | We've tried to keep you updated on what's going on at DOJ under Trump , to make every American aware of MAGA's relentless campaign, executed by the department’s political leaders, to smear the work of the longtime attorneys, dismiss noncontroversial cases, and reassign career supervisors. CNN has reported that: "During President Donald Trump’s first term, DOJ officials forced voting section attorneys to abandon the more high-profile work that had often been opposed by conservatives. But the gutting of the section during Trump’s second administration goes far beyond that, according to former department attorneys and outside voter advocates." We know our fundamental rights, including voting and so much more, aren't going to be protected by this administration. In fact, they're going to be repeatedly attacked from within our government as they try and consolidate their control forever. That's why we need to stand up and defend things from the outside. = | | | | | | ---|---|---|--- Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country. Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities. = = Contributions or gifts to Integrity First Voter Action Project are not tax deductible as charitable contributions or as business expenses under IRC Section 162(3). name: Powered by People
subject: This is unheard of in Texas
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Powered by People, [poweredxpeople
body:
#
We’ve spent the last few months recruiting candidates to run for offices up and down the ballot. Beto worked the phones, talked to community leaders about what it’s like to run, and drove across the state to hold in-person recruitment events at bars, in church meeting rooms, and in community centers.
**The goal? To have a Democrat on the ballot for every State House, State Senate, and Congressional race so we compete everywhere.**
_Take a look at the results:_
* **U.S. House:** Democrats filed to run for 38 out of 38 seats
* **State Senate:** Democrats filed to run for 16 out of 16 seats
* **State House:** Democrats filed to run for 150 out of 150 seats

This is unheard of in Texas. But it’s not enough to simply have a Democrat on the ballot in every race. Now, we need to have their backs.
[**Will you donate $3 today to help fund our on-the-ground organizing and voter registration program so that we can compete in and win races up and down the ballot in 2026?**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251209-1.8sqIww&refcode=ema20251209-240521&t=2&akid=240521%2E444211%2EPbktdE)
**[DONATE $3](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251209-1.8sqIww&refcode=ema20251209-240521&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=21&akid=240521%2E444211%2EPbktdE)**
At Powered by People, we don’t just talk about the work. We don’t just think about the work. We _do_ the work. We’re registering voters, deploying trained volunteers, holding community events, organizing on college campuses, and building the people-powered movement necessary to win in the most voter suppressed state.
[**Chip in $3 today to help us continue our work throughout Texas and ensure we can help win races up and down the ballot.**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251209-1.8sqIww&refcode=ema20251209-240521&t=22&akid=240521%2E444211%2EPbktdE)
Thank you,
Powered by People

Beto worked the phones, talked to community leaders about what it’s like to run, and drove across the state to hold in-person recruitment events at bars, in church meeting rooms, and in community centers. The goal? To have a Democrat on the ballot for every State House, State Senate, and Congressional race so we compete everywhere. Take a look at the results: U.S. House: Democrats filed to run for 38 out of 38 seats State Senate: Democrats filed to run for 16 out of 16 seats State House: Democrats filed to run for 150 out of 150 seats This is unheard of in Texas. But it’s not enough to simply have a Democrat on the ballot in every race. Now, we need to have their backs. At Powered by People, we don’t just talk about the work. We don’t just think about the work. We do the work. We’re registering voters, deploying trained volunteers, holding community events, organizing on college campuses, and building the people-powered movement necessary to win in the most voter suppressed state. Thank you, Powered by People ** Paid for by Powered by People, , not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

name: Abdul El-Sayed
subject: The elephant in the room
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Abdul for U
body:
[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/30?refcode=&t=1001&akid=1414%2E251025%2EKXnVQf ]Abdul for US Senate [ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/30?refcode=&t=1002&akid=1414%2E251025%2EKXnVQf ]Abdul for US Senate

Peter, someone at
a recent town hall asked me this:

“What really separates you from another Democrat in this primary who
eventually called what’s happening in Gaza a genocide?”

It’s a valid question, maybe one you yourself have wondered, so I want to
answer it for you today.

First, imagine I asked you, “What color is the sky?” You’d say blue.

Now, imagine I look at the same sky and say: “It’s hard to say. Could be
anything. Let me get back to you.”

Weeks go by. Then months. And finally, I say: “Okay, fine, it’s blue — but
let me give you five caveats about why maybe it’s not blue.”

You’d think that was... odd.

And what if you later learned the reason I hesitated to call the sky blue
was because I was trying to win over support from people who didn’t want
me to say what was obviously true?

That’s the difference between me and that other candidate.

When nearly 20,000 children have been killed in Gaza, that is genocide.
When people from the very place enduring the violence say it's genocide,
that is genocide.

That’s why I’ve been outspoken against sending our tax dollars overseas to
fund the genocide. I’ve never hemmed or hawed over my stance.

And I know that this will result in probably millions of dollars’ worth of
attacks from AIPAC and billionaire-backed groups. But if I can’t say
what’s true now, why should I have the job?

Michiganders have a clear choice in this primary election: Vote for a
politics that waits to see which way the wind blows — or choose a politics
rooted in moral clarity from the start.

I’ve never taken corporate PAC money. Not in 2018. Not now. Not ever.

I didn't suddenly “discover” corporate money was bad because I’m running
against someone who refuses it. I’ve been about this from day one.

But here’s the part I need your help with,
Peter:

Telling the truth comes with a real cost — and our campaign will only
withstand that pressure if we build the strongest grassroots coalition in
the country.

If thousands of us step up together, even with $5 or $10 at a time, we
replace the millions of dollars AIPAC, corporate PACs, and the old guard
will spend trying to sink this movement. And we prove that moral clarity
still has a place in American politics.

So I’m humbly asking: [ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/507?c0=em-ms-aes-20251209-1-ak.uJeZZh&amount=10&refcode=em20251209-1413&t=1004&akid=1414%2E251025%2EKXnVQf ]Will you chip in $10 or whatever
feels right to you today? Your donation is the only thing keeping this
campaign independent, honest, and unbought.

[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/507?c0=em-ms-aes-20251209-1-ak.uJeZZh&amount=10&refcode=em20251209-1413&t=1023&akid=1414%2E251025%2EKXnVQf ]Donate $10 ››

Thank you for reading this longer message, and for being a part of our
campaign.

In solidarity,

Abdul

subject: Right now, I’m madder than a wet hen!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by TEAM SCALISE, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and to benefit SCALISE FOR CONGRESS and EYE OF THE TIGER PAC
body:
[](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/jfdgrY1Fy9wZkClyoXt6oCEQq_Ucnh2QpNZR2PK4x7b_CH7_j26_iMNfJoJHPKqMpetP8cmit2A4Y0-fzqltGUw3kDvKhVN1EbUS0USeaVkmBJOgp041Mry83nqmWs-pYm1tjP-chSXIrnSqtFApMEJmRdt1LjM1qRYVXply1tfhhIMK-vuyhHqYjYS9aQLH6ogGrLbcO5_-jexqt6-MKvkIUHsGUsEKjQk7gUdHLRSvBVhF4yBNp8ZH8OfmcBJ2Ps6wyKSIFLHWO3t7Vxt8RsFHkqfpW6gdoV0LfkYx1lq_dvRz840N8aSIU7qH_FZECybwdFAoCoUi5tQ2ilyrPrRsBgxzd4sbMIfacjSHAaKUGl0hhRO03GVYuHfPh4OcOXlAFQtX8LRGX8GPIAj0BGRglX9Fvo4GNZ_lw6kpbB5w0COhqFP3_Y27K6rrx3gN7yC0165ycVLO_83YXOumNMAfwgA-ZymUz0MFHEURyLgovUgeOvM5clU8FZ8GPXYSKVdgSkypAUQVrrBCdxJpWjWK3xri3OR1KeQ_ytZDQvemxikeYMYEqffjNWgCqZLYVap3QcPBFyBfwC_sbuUTMmUlF2CWGAXhIvs1SjMSDqbloEofRke5qiZiWbfUwuzIuAs3tN_sD9-iZYdIRBpryPtlMvb-Z7DUK6cgkRjO9_yJ8S9ap4IBwB43yDGYfodjTyFXV_ftohvUgRAvqpFOv1Ai7NS5segV1X-v0NlaS-FJW_VV6iThHPuozgUIxwJKcXzyfyO3J8YcqNuDtxkIOtShpVr1td3X6hcE5_3oRFM4UCOHQDwvQ489eYNcfuF3BRM2-YO4jP34y4q0limV2vamvjRTGHM/wCwv5rU1D7o5kzYhJNE3EeaNfbamZxJT/15)The way these Democrats are acting up right now's got me madder than a wet hen in a tote sack.
This ain't the same Party that it was when I first entered the big leagues. It's become a paradise for Liberal hogwash and downright anti-American behavior.
Whether it's...

* Rioting in cities across America and burning Old Glory
* Storming ICE facilities, doxxing agents, and throwing sandwiches at law enforcement
* Having actual COMMUNISTS run some of our greatest cities

It sure seems like once you think the Democrats can't stoop any lower, they grab a shovel and prove you wrong... Guess they never heard the first rule of holes: if you're in one, STOP DIGGING.
_But let me tell you the God-honest truth. They have the money to do it and if we don 't get our act together, they just might put us in their place._
Right now, the Democrats have a well-oiled fundraising machine that is raking in the big bucks from its out-of-touch, elitist donors - 100 million bucks, to be exact.
After seeing that, I knew I had to step in.
**_[So my good friend Majority Leader Steve Scalise and I got to talking. clean_body: way these Democrats are acting up right now's got me madder than a wet hen in a tote sack. This ain't the same Party that it was when I first entered the big leagues. It's become a paradise for Liberal hogwash and downright anti-American behavior. Whether it's... Rioting in cities across America and burning Old Glory Storming ICE facilities, doxxing agents, and throwing sandwiches at law enforcement Having actual COMMUNISTS run some of our greatest cities It sure seems like once you think the Democrats can't stoop any lower, they grab a shovel and prove you wrong... Guess they never heard the first rule of holes: if you're in one, STOP DIGGING. But let me tell you the God-honest truth. They have the money to do it and if we don 't get our act together, they just might put us in their place. Right now, the Democrats have a well-oiled fundraising machine that is raking in the big bucks from its out-of-touch, elitist donors - 100 million bucks, to be exact. After seeing that, I knew I had to step in. Do you reckon you could be one of those Conservatives? we all step up and work together I think we'll be able to keep these Democrats at bay and continue to deliver wins for good, hard workin' Americans. But I can 't do it without you, Peter. Thanks for hearing me out, John N. Kennedy A personal note from Majority Leader Steve Scalise: I'm humbled to announce that I am your new House Majority Leader. name: Team Kelly
subject: Is there anything we can say?
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
body:
#

Team —
We are writing to see if there is anything — anything at all — that we can say to convince you to make a contribution to Mark Kelly before tomorrow.
What if we told you that by then, Pete Hegseth is due to receive a report that will help him decide whether he’s going to try to recall Mark to active duty and court-martial him?
What if we told you that, even if he doesn’t decide to court-martial Mark, there are any number of other forms of punishment he might choose?
What if we told you that Mark’s being singled out by Trump, Hegseth, and the FBI because they want to show they have the power to silence anyone — even a veteran, an astronaut, and a sitting senator?
And what if we told you that even now, even with everything Mark’s facing, we can prove Trump and the rest of them wrong?
This is really serious. It isn’t just about Mark, or the video, or what he said. It’s about whether we’re going to sit by and let Trump run this country like an authoritarian.
We know what side we’re on, and we’re respectfully asking you:

clean_body: Team — We are writing to see if there is anything — anything at all — that we can say to convince you to make a contribution to Mark Kelly before tomorrow. What if we told you that by then, Pete Hegseth is due to receive a report that will help him decide whether he’s going to try to recall Mark to active duty and court-martial him? What if we told you that, even if he doesn’t decide to court-martial Mark, there are any number of other forms of punishment he might choose? What if we told you that Mark’s being singled out by Trump, Hegseth, and the FBI because they want to show they have the power to silence anyone — even a veteran, an astronaut, and a sitting senator? And what if we told you that even now, even with everything Mark’s facing, we can prove Trump and the rest of them wrong? This is really serious. It isn’t just about Mark, or the video, or what he said. It’s about whether we’re going to sit by and let Trump run this country like an authoritarian. We know what side we’re on, and we’re respectfully asking you: If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly: | | ** | | | | | | | ** Thank you, Team Kelly Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate

Marni is a lifelong public servant who served overseas in the Peace Corps — and she flipped San Diego's most conservative council district blue.

Now, she's running for the U.S. House to tackle the skyrocketing cost of living, protect reproductive freedom, defend LGBTQ+ rights, and make sure Washington works for all American families — not just the wealthy few.

Meanwhile, her opponent, MAGA Republican Rep. Darrell Issa, has spent more than 20 years in Congress lining his pockets, slashing health care, and hiking costs.

Voters in California's 48th Congressional District deserve a champion who will fight for them, not against them. EMILYs List is proud to endorse Marni von Wilpert — and we're ready to help her flip this seat and take the fight to Congress.

name: Andrea Salinas
subject: ICE tried lying to my FACE
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Andrea Salinas for Oregon
body:
This is not justice. This is terror.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120943520/587858391/-1080040549?refcode=email-header&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0M2UwNWY3ZS0zNGQ1LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Ds_MU2bOrohcJFmP-1Eqikv3XBPUUPH4SlwcmWMNL-o=) Team, I had to reach out. I recently visited the Tacoma, Washington ICE facility, and ICE tried to _**lie**_ to my FACE. For the past many months, Trump’s cruel abuse of ICE’s authority has terrorized our communities and disappeared so many of our friends, family, and loved ones. I wanted to see these ICE facilities myself and understand what my constituents, and so many other members of our community, are being put through. _**When I asked about the conditions, ICE tried to lie to my face.**_ When the Salinas family came to the United States from Mexico in 1945, we did so because the United States was the land of opportunity. Donald Trump and MAGA extremists are tearing apart that dream. **This is not justice._This is terror._ And I will not let Trump, or any Republican standing with him, get away with this.** _**[Flipping the House blue in 2026 could be our only line of defense against Trump and his MAGA agenda. Our work to flip the House, and put lawmakers in charge who will ACTUALLY stand up to hold this authoritarian administration accountable to the American people has NEVER been more — and that starts with defending must-win seats like OR-06. If you’re with me, then please, can I count on you to rush a donation to my campaign?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120943521/587858392/1477919608?amount=6&refcode=email_120925_icelied&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0M2UwNWY3ZS0zNGQ1LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Ds_MU2bOrohcJFmP-1Eqikv3XBPUUPH4SlwcmWMNL-o=)**_ _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | | [Donate $6 →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120943522/587858393/1477919608?amount=6&express_lane=true&refcode=email_120925_icelied&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0M2UwNWY3ZS0zNGQ1LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Ds_MU2bOrohcJFmP-1Eqikv3XBPUUPH4SlwcmWMNL-o=)

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

[Click here to see what RFK Jr is inspiring](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=a8e19c8f46e44511a4b166caa4d2f196&_e=rPKdugf8tyPeC6GksBUW54iqNc831WVWdxNZL79HHEzg7OQSdLab3p2lpYOcetaFLZ_DGdlu5LPiePRU3n8rcujm0AUbi_g7IxuMvDGz06_AkhqV6IoYKMwR18lZNeMYWAntVBcKF9rfINKcxGFfo99n3xwLYnB0MbvQjW4czFbkt2oyae8-ePh91safTDnpNr7vzRB7wuoLHr5QpYl4QU3rPHDIbQUFNR2XaTPJy4Peyn5tAHQSh8muFLjvacK2Tt8iYB75cTS1YtjP3gWQK6m5NEfKT4LqtgtawD_Qg4sdxs489GowYbe1HWyCgT1KJ617lJX4hZGFiF-WENwhN6zo-q47oPazreca20dXSAVoptsKQabcv5QHAgffcwKTIcZXBTweu7V4re59m7Ew2aUwbFRrGOyotyVosajg3wCqZ5goHHXk90SN2GFVvVSoCE4HtZHjCbenPXQaWkGo77Ray4Js9WqxrwBg45VFRvNB4L4kAOeODvcM9qoFV99s0nQUGO3vA8iemCMqipnGFgDbgJms-EtsFryO1hSZM9rrfSqzbC8Qs59xmF2iTW6T).
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from Your Capital Minds (YCM), an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. body:

We’re in your inbox today to share just three reasons why you should chip in $3 to Sherrod Brown’s Senate campaign today.
Sherrod is fighting for working people, not Wall Street.
Experts at The Cook Political Report have increased his chances of winning in Ohio.
Corporate special interests will spend millions to defeat him. Last cycle, Ohio’s race was the most expensive Senate race in history.
Sherrod is running a grassroots campaign, and its success depends on folks like you. These are three good reasons to donate, so now, all we can do is ask:

subject: Put American citizens first
disclaimer_text: paid for by Arkansans and Americans
body:
I have a plan| | | [](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=6afa204e6ed940d29104e65e50517c28&_e=gEE3s6_EePywyE0mxSGeFAIyFHgCOj8lTnxXjUAS_PozKj3wOsznVqCcviBbFxwVWvrkJ0mYkVvAYU0a8dp-uLcIIp2Hetb2IFwZhu4wrayROspvnCrs_ljjm3_vubE2cDQFSO8gN0NU72RTKjEzPoXGXlMRoaF0Nn8JboslFW5pDtg8WtssgcnE5P1spPzsn1bl4r7DihXJmnuHepJUyk3NXGgi66CxBwllLzPH2DxywPZV3QogHpArBuCtvnMQ5NTk3DQonycGOpJZsEbBdy1orpcfTzIj91BTAJuzdUBBivkJ9q2splbX2rd55rT2V1de4JDfLYED6ZRqIFpBS1lXZh9RwhKpZxc-a11Fm89MdfQRQsu2cXvjeig4h95wk0BFVP4RrL-2u54H7n3HZu1P3CZtEinIW2v1oDM3GkDQ_0Od6cmieG__5w_jDcInmc_P4G1g1XAyPOTQ3SwVtNefRV8D7GkzFSM01-ap287tro0Rj9QGQPuCazks4cc9WsCB3jkFLinm75_bp-dcBW5B466GlFjFGxRvizoXaesMj-qsE5ehPjdBIS2qHgFcy6VPY2OaVaXxJBQAFS1Q3RSYBbTeyotnzRWr_BuerifR-zHArQJrSBcxW5QIApWmE4A0jvqPqj8vqZbhwtXT18qvNR2-lpoRxD2TUtO0MTgKbA_048_aMfr-wd8mPwpsa_W_rpqXDVA3SnMbsTW6zM5pIGoOnrL5TOD0E1G45T0%3D)
---
Non-citizens shouldn’t be getting federal benefits paid for by Arkansans and Americans. And any naturalized person who commits acts of violence, threatens our system of government, or participates in a riot should have their citizenship stripped. I have a plan to stop your tax dollars from being used to give benefits to non-citizens and to put Americans first. [**__Will you back me up on it? SIGN your name here.__**](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=6afa204e6ed940d29104e65e50517c28&_e=gEE3s6_EePywyE0mxSGeFAIyFHgCOj8lTnxXjUAS_PozKj3wOsznVqCcviBbFxwVWvrkJ0mYkVvAYU0a8dp-uLcIIp2Hetb2IFwZhu4wrayROspvnCrs_ljjm3_vubE2cDQFSO8gN0NU72RTKjEzPoXGXlMRoaF0Nn8JboslFW5pDtg8WtssgcnE5P1spPzsn1bl4r7DihXJmnuHepJUyk3NXGgi66CxBwllLzPH2DxywPZV3QogHpArBuCtvnMQq6oMAczTLxoZDcZsewGvsLlYsbEU1mrrewt-aXmviLb2Lx2ga9elMcuRw9WvkGLbMInVsPvPMArmeyICvJZdMgYz5v-XflC-YAl7LYqsUkewJBoMp3ygkODSyZ-mng_TaCGwiO-Of5jLaIfb5Tncg5VrpFnFFgDK7QBw3bP6nRGhS1VYi4qTFa-Ke7xvpuTeUMnJdXHoq9ROJ1vtp-TdAb16jr7ZLj7ZDNg9cEfBXJPl6I0ijkvqHedVIoI-5pxMdW6QHaHIxQ5S20Eoqbpp5xsa49TDj_GPdIn252FIriYsXzF1k3f7LB_7OLVblUpJBFnCcejDM6IDoGJxZgBuAlxm3NS7cpQLE9JDcfPngPnJKnqO55vK69rW68KvBqv3iHnofYDKjtAp09NLemGJ1l7LjnlCJgyqr3qkFoH7koQiTYM-YUemrOS2ivN7m3yDiJKaG8O9KTWH0tczVJPF1plIxeARKRbyAQZ1vXqtpRo%3D)

# [The Fight Enters Its Final Stretch](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3260111&post_id=181167912&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgxMTY3OTEyLCJpYXQiOjE3NjUzMDk0MzksImV4cCI6MTc2NzkwMTQzOSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMyNjAxMTEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.cArVmm_bnIqr2HvqmwTSZikfk6kXttd1FYEAG-hvfgI)
### The Court gives a timeline on Zahid's case - as conditions inside drive detainees to slit their wrists.
| | [Melissa Chaudhry](https://substack.com/@melissa4congress)
---
| Dec 9

Over the weekend, two people tried to kill themselves in Zahid's unit.
(His unit is approximately 1/16th of the people held in that facility.)
Slashed at their wrists, apparently, with whatever they could find that held an edge.
The guards intervened and took them to medical before they could fully escape the concentration camp by escaping their lives --
But that is what this is doing to human beings.
* * *
On Sunday evening, I took Salma in to see her Baba. She cradles the phone and gazes at him, tells him she loves him, tells him stories, asks him to tell her stories, listens with her whole heart. Sometimes she leans her head against the glass toward his hand. When he said "I love you soooo much, I wish I could hug you right now" she responded, "Baba, you can't hug me, you're behind the window."
At home, she lines up her toys with great focus and tells me "I'm working to help get Baba home." (Video at the bottom of this post.) She asks me daily, "Has the judge decided yet?"
* * *
Make it make sense.
* * *
**Court update:** on Friday, we put in our 25-year record of Zahid's targeting, ever since he refused to become an informant for the FBI. Key points:
* The "final order of removal" from 2008 was issued under a discriminatory scheme (CARRP) now formally judged unlawful.
* It relied on faulty pretexts at the time - including testimony from one Lt. Gary Belles of the Yakima Police Department, who framed Zahid as "falsely claiming citizenship" ...but later admitted _under oath_ during deposition that Zahid had done no such thing.
* One of the exhibits we included was excerpts from Belles's deposition proving that his "grounds" the removal order was based on was false. Sworn false. Also proving that the opposition has criminally failed in its duty to update the record - they knew Belles was untrustworthy well over a decade ago.
* Belles was also the subject of a public Yakima scandal for using racist epithets in public while in uniform - standing in line at a Starbucks referring to Hispanic people as "wetbacks" and "beaners" and opining that more Japanese people should have been killed in WWII.
* The Judge at the Western District of Washington can formally determine that the '08 "order of removal" cannot be used as the basis for detention.
* That would be a big win. Their biggest and most consistent argument - and the argument the Immigration Judge used to decide she couldn't award him bond - is that he "has a final order of removal." If that's ruled to be so fatally flawed (as well as ancient, stale, actively litigated for the last 17 years, AND currently the subject of a federal stay of removal at the 9th Circuit) as to not be relevant to justify detention, their case falls apart.

* * *
Since Friday, much has happened. Respondents - which include Pam Bondi and Kristi Noem, as well as the Warden of NWIPC Bruce Scott, and ICE-ERO Seattle Field Office Director Laura Hermosillo (formerly Bostock) - filed their "Notice of Intent to Oppose," saying that they're going to actively argue against letting Zahid go.
On Saturday - yes, the Court is working this on an emergency basis, including through weekends - Respondents submitted their Reply and three Declarations, which should reach Zahid today.
On Sunday the 7th, the Judge made an order laying out the timeline for this case:
* Respondents have until the 10th - _tomorrow_ \- to put in any further material they want considered.
* Following that, Zahid has until the 17th to file a reply, addressing their arguments.
* As soon as possible following the 17th, there will be a hearing. Zahid will be video-conferenced in from the detention center. (_I do not know the logistics of that yet, or whether we can come pack the courtroom. I 'll keep you posted._)
* Promptly - I presume - following the hearing, the Judge will issue his ruling.
* Our judge in this case is an experienced litigator who spent ten years in his own immigration law practice, including representing detained clients. He knows this world intimately, and he's been responsive and reasonable so far. We're deeply grateful.

While the rest of our communities are wrapped up in holiday parties, winter fundraisers, school break, and all the rest.... we'll be here, fighting for Zahid's freedom.
* * *
The Judge's order did several other important things:
* It required Respondents to transmit copies of their filings to the Warden at NWIPC, with instructions for him to transmit them to Zahid. No more 8-day delays waiting for the mail. This is very important.
* It stated that the Court will treat this as an "Expedited Habeas" request and decide _both_ the request for emergency release _and_ the underlying lawfulness or unlawfulness of detention at the same time. That's good, I think.
* It mandated that Zahid **not be moved** without a full 48 hours written notice to the Court, _not counting_ weekends and federal holidays. They are explicitly prohibited from disappearing my husband out of jurisdiction in the middle of the night, the way they have so many others.

* * *
Another important development: they have started administering an immunosuppressant medication to him, in a weak attempt to address his eye condition. It's making his eyes worse, however, and triggering severe side effects that the pharmacy tech _inside_ identified as an allergic reaction that requires him to stop taking it.
This immunosuppressant has apparently been tried in this particular condition - on a whopping 72 people... (medication trials are considered robust if it's thousands...) of whom about 40 said that maybe it helped somewhat.
Experimental medication, with a low usefulness, triggering severe reactions, that _suppresses his immune system_ in a place that's filthy and crowded, as a cheapo "intervention" so they can get out of giving him the care he **needs** to keep from going blind? Give me a break.
* * *
So, here we are. My husband - a decorated, disabled veteran, who has peacefully and lawfully lived in this country for 25 years, who's never broken any US laws, who's a father to two toddlers and a loving husband, who's a pillar of the community and a mentor to so many - is trapped in a place where grown men can't stop crying for days and try to slash their wrists to escape. Experimental medication is being pushed on him that actively makes things worse, makes him more likely to get sick, and won't address the underlying problem. He's still, chronically, underfed and sleep deprived and hyper-policed, in loud, crowded, chaotic conditions.
**And Pam Bondi, Kristi Noem, and the rest are** _**actively arguing**_**to keep him there.**
I'll let you know how it goes.
Take care out there,
Melissa

Sometimes she leans her head against the glass toward his hand. When he said "I love you soooo much, I wish I could hug you right now" she responded, "Baba, you can't hug me, you're behind the window." At home, she lines up her toys with great focus and tells me "I'm working to help get Baba home." (Video at the bottom of this post.) She asks me daily, "Has the judge decided yet?" Make it make sense. Court update: on Friday, we put in our 25-year record of Zahid's targeting, ever since he refused to become an informant for the FBI. Key points: The "final order of removal" from 2008 was issued under a discriminatory scheme (CARRP) now formally judged unlawful. It relied on faulty pretexts at the time - including testimony from one Lt. Gary Belles of the Yakima Police Department, who framed Zahid as "falsely claiming citizenship" ...but later admitted under oath during deposition that Zahid had done no such thing. One of the exhibits we included was excerpts from Belles's deposition proving that his "grounds" the removal order was based on was false. Sworn false. Also proving that the opposition has criminally failed in its duty to update the record - they knew Belles was untrustworthy well over a decade ago. Belles was also the subject of a public Yakima scandal for using racist epithets in public while in uniform - standing in line at a Starbucks referring to Hispanic people as "wetbacks" and "beaners" and opining that more Japanese people should have been killed in WWII. The Judge at the Western District of Washington can formally determine that the '08 "order of removal" cannot be used as the basis for detention. That would be a big win. Their biggest and most consistent argument - and the argument the Immigration Judge used to decide she couldn't award him bond - is that he "has a final order of removal." If that's ruled to be so fatally flawed (as well as ancient, stale, actively litigated for the last 17 years, AND currently the subject of a federal stay of removal at the 9th Circuit) as to not be relevant to justify detention, their case falls apart. Since Friday, much has happened. Respondents - which include Pam Bondi and Kristi Noem, as well as the Warden of NWIPC Bruce Scott, and ICE-ERO Seattle Field Office Director Laura Hermosillo (formerly Bostock) - filed their "Notice of Intent to Oppose," saying that they're going to actively argue against letting Zahid go. On Saturday - yes, the Court is working this on an emergency basis, including through weekends - Respondents submitted their Reply and three Declarations, which should reach Zahid today. On Sunday the 7th, the Judge made an order laying out the timeline for this case: Respondents have until the 10th - tomorrow \- to put in any further material they want considered. Following that, Zahid has until the 17th to file a reply, addressing their arguments. As soon as possible following the 17th, there will be a hearing. Zahid will be video-conferenced in from the detention center. (I do not know the logistics of that yet, or whether we can come pack the courtroom. I 'll keep you posted.) Promptly - I presume - following the hearing, the Judge will issue his ruling. Our judge in this case is an experienced litigator who spent ten years in his own immigration law practice, including representing detained clients. He knows this world intimately, and he's been responsive and reasonable so far. We're deeply grateful. While the rest of our communities are wrapped up in holiday parties, winter fundraisers, school break, and all the rest.... we'll be here, fighting for Zahid's freedom. The Judge's order did several other important things: It required Respondents to transmit copies of their filings to the Warden at NWIPC, with instructions for him to transmit them to Zahid. No more 8-day delays waiting for the mail. This is very important. It stated that the Court will treat this as an "Expedited Habeas" request and decide both the request for emergency release and the underlying lawfulness or unlawfulness of detention at the same time. That's good, I think. It mandated that Zahid not be moved without a full 48 hours written notice to the Court, not counting weekends and federal holidays. They are explicitly prohibited from disappearing my husband out of jurisdiction in the middle of the night, the way they have so many others. Another important development: they have started administering an immunosuppressant medication to him, in a weak attempt to address his eye condition. It's making his eyes worse, however, and triggering severe side effects that the pharmacy tech inside identified as an allergic reaction that requires him to stop taking it. This immunosuppressant has apparently been tried in this particular condition - on a whopping 72 people... (medication trials are considered robust if it's thousands...) of whom about 40 said that maybe it helped somewhat. Experimental medication, with a low usefulness, triggering severe reactions, that suppresses his immune system in a place that's filthy and crowded, as a cheapo "intervention" so they can get out of giving him the care he needs to keep from going blind? Give me a break. So, here we are. My husband - a decorated, disabled veteran, who has peacefully and lawfully lived in this country for 25 years, who's never broken any US laws, who's a father to two toddlers and a loving husband, who's a pillar of the community and a mentor to so many - is trapped in a place where grown men can't stop crying for days and try to slash their wrists to escape. Experimental medication is being pushed on him that actively makes things worse, makes him more likely to get sick, and won't address the underlying problem. He's still, chronically, underfed and sleep deprived and hyper-policed, in loud, crowded, chaotic conditions. And Pam Bondi, Kristi Noem, and the rest are actively arguingto keep him there.** I'll let you know how it goes. Take care out there, Melissa | | ---|---|--- You 're currently a free subscriber to "It's a Matter of Integrity" by Melissa Chaudhry. To support the mission and access the full experience, upgrade your subscription. | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Melissa Chaudhry 6727 Martin Luther King Jr Way S, Suite M, #1117 Seattle, WA 98118 ? ?

name: Sign Your Name | Left Action
subject: Tell Reporters to Stop Treating Trump with Respect He Doesn't Deserve
body:
| | | | |
---
| | |
# [Tell Reporters to Stop Kissing Trump's Ass, and Demand He Answer Tough Questions](https://default.salsalabs.org/T39a9fc6b-7737-4862-9db0-c29c7d004615/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T0eff04ee-9cc4-4627-9343-250afdee3d44/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | | _([Click here to go straight to action](https://default.salsalabs.org/T4d0c1f48-4d6b-4c09-9c18-12fef3e0559b/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9). More background and discussion on this at [our Substack page](https://default.salsalabs.org/T943708fc-645c-4262-b346-5a53f95bab52/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)! )_ We’ve seen this humiliating ritual time and time again: 1\. Reporter asks Trump a question, and uses the deferential tone normally reserved for a Dickens character meeting the Queen. “Beggin’ your pardon, your majesty (bows), I am but a humble chimney sweep and fish monger not worthy of breathing the same air as you (bows again), but might I please, please pretty please ask a tiny little question?” (bows even lower) 2\. Trump responds exactly as Trump does: with rage, insults, lies, and nothing resembling an actual answer. 3\. And then the reporter either:
a) doesn’t follow up, or
b) follows up in the soft-focus tone of a children’s librarian. **“Your majesty, you said the Earth is flat, which is a **_**difference of opinion**_** from **_**some**_** scientists...”** So the cycle continues. Too many reporters are still using the old rules, treating Trump as if he were a normal president, deserving respect. But he’s not.

**[He’s a dick. A free-range dick, going wherever he wants on the truth and subject spectrums.](https://default.salsalabs.org/T8ae6c611-3534-44e5-8352-8b9717940cb7/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)** And he gets away with it because we’re supposed to be polite and deferential to the president.

The only way to get a real answer from Trump is to aggressively call him out on his bullshit in real time, and, if necessary, to be a dick right back:

_“Mr. President, you’re obviously lying, and everyone knows it. Are you not able to answer the question?”

“Mr. President, you are obviously full of shit. Do you need an enema before continuing with the press conference?”

“Mr. President, you said something today, then said the exact opposite thing 5 minutes later, and then denied it. Did you have a stroke?”_

We need to make him look like a fool when he doesn’t answer the question. And we need to make him look weak — the one thing that his supporters won’t forgive. **We get that it’s incredibly hard to be a reporter dealing with Trump, and big kudos to those trying to get the truth, risking the wrath of him and his supporters.**

But the old way is no longer working, and it’s time to take a new approach.And the next time Trump steamrolls a press conference, reporters must remember: **Democracy doesn’t need more deference.
It needs a spine.
It needs pushback.
It needs truth spoken out loud, in real time, without apology.** If the press won’t fight back, authoritarianism wins by default. [**Don’t let that happen. Take Action!**](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tff776c52-953e-4bf6-b1bd-51850edb1102/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---

Reporter asks Trump a question, and uses the deferential tone normally reserved for a Dickens character meeting the Queen. “Beggin’ your pardon, your majesty (bows), I am but a humble chimney sweep and fish monger not worthy of breathing the same air as you (bows again), but might I please, please pretty please ask a tiny little question?” (bows even lower) 2\. Trump responds exactly as Trump does: with rage, insults, lies, and nothing resembling an actual answer. 3\. And then the reporter either: a) doesn’t follow up, or b) follows up in the soft-focus tone of a children’s librarian. “Your majesty, you said the Earth is flat, which is a difference of opinion from some scientists...” So the cycle continues. Too many reporters are still using the old rules, treating Trump as if he were a normal president, deserving respect. But he’s not. And he gets away with it because we’re supposed to be polite and deferential to the president. The only way to get a real answer from Trump is to aggressively call him out on his bullshit in real time, and, if necessary, to be a dick right back: “Mr. President, you’re obviously lying, and everyone knows it. Are you not able to answer the question?” “Mr. President, you are obviously full of shit. Do you need an enema before continuing with the press conference?” “Mr. President, you said something today, then said the exact opposite thing 5 minutes later, and then denied it. Did you have a stroke?” We need to make him look like a fool when he doesn’t answer the question. And we need to make him look weak — the one thing that his supporters won’t forgive. We get that it’s incredibly hard to be a reporter dealing with Trump, and big kudos to those trying to get the truth, risking the wrath of him and his supporters. But the old way is no longer working, and it’s time to take a new approach.And the next time Trump steamrolls a press conference, reporters must remember: Democracy doesn’t need more deference. It needs a spine. It needs pushback. It needs truth spoken out loud, in real time, without apology. If the press won’t fight back, authoritarianism wins by default. | | | | | | | Thank you, Left Action | | | | | | Other things you can do: Follow us on * Follow Us | | LeftAction Washington, District of Columbia info@leftaction.com | Having trouble viewing this email? | | | or

subject: Never expected to get the cold shoulder from YOU
body:

I know I’ve reached out this week about this issue.My team tells me they’ve texted and emailed at least twice too!
Is everything ok? Should we take the hint and assume you aren’t interested in these messages?
That would be truly shocking to me since you’ve always been such a steadfast supporter of mine and President Trump’s mission to save America, so I’m giving you one more chance!

That’s how we build the grassroots power needed to protect reproductive healthcare access, workers’ rights, voting rights, and the future of our democracy.
