| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---
Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

# [Trump Broke the Economy -- And Now It's Breaking Him](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4258115&post_id=181173292&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgxMTczMjkyLCJpYXQiOjE3NjUzMTA4ODAsImV4cCI6MTc2NzkwMjg4MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQyNTgxMTUiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Q46zQ1vxBZ_bdDk-1iJy9aClkV07s0K6Y9WkKOJdg7o)
### Trump's lies don't pay the rent.
| | [Mike Nellis](https://substack.com/@mikenellis)
---
| Dec 9
---
| [](https://substack.com/@mikenellis)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4258115&post_id=181173292&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgxMTczMjkyLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2NTMxMDg4MCwiZXhwIjoxNzY3OTAyODgwLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNDI1ODExNSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.tTy73T8e4ZNB4V9rgS1yyz2RGVSN2RvoMDYaFoar4aI&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/endlessurgency/p/trump-broke-the-economy-and-now-its?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Donald Trump's approval rating has collapsed because of the economy -- and the White House knows it. That's why they've got him out on the road tonight in Pennsylvania (at a casino, no less), trying to reassure people about an affordability crisis he still refuses to admit exists.
Trump won't acknowledge that prices are crushing families. He won't acknowledge that the job market is getting gutted by AI and his tariffs. He won't acknowledge that millions of Americans feel like they're falling behind every single month. Because the minute he acknowledges it, he has to acknowledge something else:
He caused it.
* * *
_**[Endless Urgency is free to read, but we're hustling to launch our new content studio and expand our team with a video editor. If you've been enjoying the work, now's the time to back it. Paid subscribers make it possible--just $8/month makes a huge difference: ](https://substack.com/redirect/98cfbb7b-7614-4ff0-82fc-421cfcc0a8a8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/98cfbb7b-7614-4ff0-82fc-421cfcc0a8a8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
His approval rating isn't tanking because people suddenly became Democrats. It's tanking because people know when they're getting screwed. They can tell the difference between political spin and the number staring back at them when they check their bank account.
I watched Trump at his press conference yesterday announcing the $12 billion bailout for American farmers, where he insisted everything was great. No crisis. No problem. Nothing to see here. The economy is "booming." We're in a "golden age." The problem isn't just that he's lying -- it's that the lie is so insulting.
Americans don't need a lecture about how well they're doing. They need a president who understands what they're up against and is going to do something about it.
A functional political party would hear what voters said in Virginia and New Jersey when affordability became the defining issue of those blowout elections. They'd reset. They'd deliver. Hell, at least they'd try. But the Republican Party can't do any of that because it's not a party -- it's a dependency. Trump will say "everything is fine" tonight, and everyone else in his party has to pretend the house isn't on fire. When one man controls the message, the message can't change -- even when the country demands it.
Tens of millions of Americans are struggling, and Trump still talks like the crisis is imaginary. But this isn't a crisis you can bullshit your way through. People aren't angry because of vibes -- they're angry because their paychecks no longer cover the basics. Every grocery run, every rent payment, every student loan bill is a reminder that the economy is not what Trump says it is.
This is the first time in his political life -- maybe outside the Epstein files -- where Trump can't gaslight his way out. People know what things cost. They know what their wages look like. They know what jobs exist and what jobs don't. The stock market means nothing to most Americans, especially when all the growth is concentrated in AI and speculative tech. That isn't prosperity -- it's a bubble.
And bubbles don't pay the rent.
* * *
_**[Endless Urgency is free to read, but we're hustling to launch our new content studio and expand our team with a video editor. If you've been enjoying the work, now's the time to back it. Paid subscribers make it possible--just $8/month makes a huge difference: ](https://substack.com/redirect/98cfbb7b-7614-4ff0-82fc-421cfcc0a8a8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/98cfbb7b-7614-4ff0-82fc-421cfcc0a8a8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
Let's talk about the job market he refuses to discuss. AI isn't some distant threat -- it's hollowing out the entry-level job market right now. Companies are freezing hiring across entire sectors because automation lets them. If you're 22, 23, 24, fresh out of school trying to start your life, you are facing the weakest job market for young workers in a generation -- and Trump has no plan, no policy, not even a sentence acknowledging it.
Instead, he's doing the opposite. Yesterday, he signed an executive order making it harder for states to regulate AI. He doesn't understand what AI is doing to the job market--or he doesn't care. Pick your poison on that one.
Add to that his tariffs -- the tax hikes that raise prices on everything from food to appliances to building materials. Tariffs hit consumers, full stop. They drive inflation. Trump knows it -- that's why he's quietly easing some of them while publicly denying they raise prices. It's economic hypocrisy in plain sight.
Republican candidates want to run on affordability. They want to talk about real issues. They want to tell voters the truth. They can't. Telling the truth requires contradicting Trump, and contradicting Trump is forbidden, ask Marjorie Taylor Greene. The entire GOP is trapped in a political physics problem: they can't fix the economy because they can't break from the man who broke it.
So instead of solutions, we get theater.
Take yesterday's $12 billion farmer bailout. Trump sat there listening to executives praise him for "saving" farmers -- even though his own tariffs are the reason farmers needed saving in the first place. It's the same pattern every time: he creates the crisis, then demands applause for handing out a bandage. Like celebrating an arsonist for putting out the fire.
Some farmers may appreciate the bailout, and I get that. But most would rather sell their products at fair prices, at home and abroad, without needing a government rescue.
Trump's inner circle keeps him insulated and delusional because facing the truth would mean admitting his economic agenda is collapsing -- taking his presidency along with it. So no matter what Trump says tonight in Pennsylvania -- no matter how hard he insists the economy is thriving -- nothing he says actually fixes the problem. The more he talks without acting, the angrier people get. And the angrier people get, the clearer the political consequences become.
Trump built his myth on being the businessman who understood the economy. That myth -- and it was always just a myth -- is dead. He doesn't understand this economy. He doesn't understand what people are living through. He doesn't understand that denial isn't a strategy -- it's a confession.
And that's why I can't wait for the midterms next year. Because the economy Trump broke is the economy that's going to break his presidency.

Peter, **Should an illegal alien who can 't read our road signs, can't speak English, and won't even give us his name be allowed to drive an 80,000lbs truck on our roads?** There's _no way_ that person should be behind the wheel of a semi-truck. But in leftist states like California, that's perfectly fine. **These are real driver 's licenses from liberal states across the country.****** This should OUTRAGE you. It outrages me.

What's worse? This madness has begun to turn deadly.
Even here in Florida -- the state that's become America's model for enforcing immigration laws -- three of our citizens are dead because an illegal alien, licensed in California, drove an 80,000-pound truck into their path.

body:
A runaway train is barreling toward our country.
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120946581/587869358/2140832878?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTQxMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhYjYxNzZiZi0zYmQ1LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=mAVBiNc7V6hOQgL8GJq7sbm_UP3KXk3blT64bN9PnlM=&emci=37c371c1-25d5-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=ab6176bf-3bd5-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=9818364)
---

| | A runaway train is barreling toward our country. Earlier this year, Republicans voted to raise monthly health insurance premiums for tens of millions of families across the country. For years, Republicans have been targeting the Affordable Care Act, with seemingly no plan to replace the coverage that millions of families would lose. Donald Trump and Congressional Republicans repeatedly claim that they have _"a plan,"_ but all they have to offer is concepts of a plan. Earlier this year, I spoke with a couple in Eugene, Oregon, who will see their monthly premiums go from around $400 a month to over $2200 a month. That's a price increase so staggering it leaves you speechless. No one in the United States of America should be expected to pay what amounts to a second mortgage for their health insurance. Millions of families just like this one will be forced to make tough choices between keeping their health care and covering their rent and groceries. There's a simple solution to stop this runaway train barreling toward our country: Republicans need to join Democrats and vote for our legislation that will stop this price increase from going into effect on January 1st. **By refusing to vote for our bill to keep costs down, Republicans are pushing millions of families to the edge of an economic cliff.** From their trillion-dollar blow to Medicaid to voting to raise premiums, 2025 has been the year Republicans took a sledgehammer to health care in America. I'm under no illusions that our system is perfect. I think we can all agree that health care costs and insurance companies are spinning out of control, and more needs to be done to rein them in. But the solution is not kicking millions of people off their health care. That's why I'm working around the clock to keep costs down for families _now_. But then it's time to drastically overhaul the for-profit system that puts profits over patients. More soon, _Ron_ | Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution to Ron Wyden instantly:

clean_body: A runaway train is barreling toward our country. | | | | A runaway train is barreling toward our country. Earlier this year, Republicans voted to raise monthly health insurance premiums for tens of millions of families across the country. For years, Republicans have been targeting the Affordable Care Act, with seemingly no plan to replace the coverage that millions of families would lose. Donald Trump and Congressional Republicans repeatedly claim that they have "a plan," but all they have to offer is concepts of a plan. Earlier this year, I spoke with a couple in Eugene, Oregon, who will see their monthly premiums go from around $400 a month to over $2200 a month. That's a price increase so staggering it leaves you speechless. No one in the United States of America should be expected to pay what amounts to a second mortgage for their health insurance. Millions of families just like this one will be forced to make tough choices between keeping their health care and covering their rent and groceries. There's a simple solution to stop this runaway train barreling toward our country: Republicans need to join Democrats and vote for our legislation that will stop this price increase from going into effect on January 1st. By refusing to vote for our bill to keep costs down, Republicans are pushing millions of families to the edge of an economic cliff. From their trillion-dollar blow to Medicaid to voting to raise premiums, 2025 has been the year Republicans took a sledgehammer to health care in America. I'm under no illusions that our system is perfect. I think we can all agree that health care costs and insurance companies are spinning out of control, and more needs to be done to rein them in. But the solution is not kicking millions of people off their health care. That's why I'm working around the clock to keep costs down for families now. But then it's time to drastically overhaul the for-profit system that puts profits over patients. More soon, Ron | Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution to Ron Wyden instantly: | = | = | = | = ---|---|---|--- | | Paid for and authorized by Wyden for Senate. | This email was sent by Wyden for Senate P.O. Box 3498 Portland, OR 97208 United States Email is an important way for Ron Wyden to keep supporters like you informed about critical issues and to build a winning grassroots campaign. Too much email? or . Questions or concerns? . |

clean_body: Background on the Justice Department's effort to indict James Comey [NEWSLINK] BREAKING NEWS... [600x300] [] DOJ dealt setback in effort to reindict Comey [] Source: The Hill [] A federal judge agreed Saturday to temporarily block Justice Department access to emails from a close associate of James Comey, issuing another roadblock as prosecutors weigh bringing a second case against the former FBI director. [13x13x1] [] READ MORE [] Background Notes from Judicial Watch [180x150x1] [] November 20, 2025 Judicial Watch Sues Justice Department for FBI Records Found in Secret Room [] Source: Judicial Watch [] Judicial Watch filed a Freedom of Information Act lawsuit against the Department of Justice for “hidden” records from former FBI Director James Comey’s era that were referenced during a Fox News interview with Deputy Director Dan Bongino. [13x13x1] [] READ MORE [] [180x150x2] [] November 25, 2025 Russiagate: The Comey Conspiracy [] Source: Judicial Watch [] In September, former FBI Director James Comey was indicted on charges of false statements and obstruction of Congress; a federal judge threw out the case this week but Attorney General Pam Bondi vowed “an immediate appeal.” [13x13x1] [] READ MORE [] [180x150x3] [] September 25, 2025 Judicial Watch Statement on the Indictment of Former FBI Director James Comey [] Source: Judicial Watch [] Judicial Watch President Tom Fitton: "Comey’s crimes are legion, but this indictment is one small step toward holding accountable those who engaged in the worst political abuse in the history of the country. name: Demand Justice PAC
subject: Here are the Senate Democrats who betrayed our courts
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Demand Justice PAC
body:
[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/54940?refcode=em20251209-60977&t=1001&akid=60977%2E1407376%2EV09dyd ]Demand Justice PAC[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/54940?refcode=em20251209-60977&t=1002&akid=60977%2E1407376%2EV09dyd ]Demand Justice PAC

ICYMI — our courts are
facing a full-scale takeover.

Trump is stacking the judiciary with judges who refuse to acknowledge
reality and instead echo his falsehoods. This isn’t hypothetical — it’s
happening now, and these Senate Democrats are helping him along the way:

Coons, Durbin, Fetterman, Gallego, Hassan, Heinrich, Hirono, Kaine, Kelly,
King, Klobuchar, Peters, Reed, Rosen, Schiff, Shaheen, Welch, and
Whitehouse.

The reality of these nominees is that not a single one will admit Joe
Biden won the 2020 election, nearly all dodge the truth about January 6,
calling it a mere “political controversy,” and every nominee repeats the
same rehearsed talking points instead of giving honest answers.

This isn’t just partisan politics. Confirming these judges hands unchecked
power to Trump loyalists and threatens protections for voting rights,
civil rights, reproductive freedom, and the rule of law. The courts should
serve the people, not the President and his billionaire buddies.

Demand Justice’s No Crony Judges campaign is taking a stand: Senate
Democrats must vote NO on every nominee who refuses to defend our
judiciary — or be held accountable.

Everything is on the line,

Demand Justice PAC

Our courts are being hijacked. Trump is filling the judiciary with

name: Team Lindstrom
subject: Democrats new target just dropped
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Brett Lindstrom for Congress
body:
The Left thinks they can buy this seat. Prove them wrong.
| | | | Peter, We need to level with you.

National Democrats just reclassified Nebraska’s 2nd Congressional District as one of their **top pickup opportunities** and they’re openly talking about how “easy” it will be to flip this seat.
That should concern every conservative in Nebraska.
Here’s what this really means:
* The Left believes NE-02 is **ripe for a takeover**.

* They see Brett’s race as a **prime opportunity to expand their majority**.

Yesterday, I filed to run for Attorney General at the Republican Party of Texas's Austin office.

I could serve the 21st Congressional District of Texas for a lifetime and be professionally fulfilled. **But representatives should not be permanent.**

_It's time for a change._

I am ready to come home to Texas. I want to take my experience in Congress as a federal prosecutor and First Assistant Attorney General to fight for Texas.

But I cannot do this alone, Fellow Patriot. It doesn't need to be a big donation - **[even just $5 will help us continue this movement.](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=51df806151344e2790942d3410187a48&_e=0IUqC2b3GmrCDYL_dVtjSM4lYOfL8QvZfwNqshfD4Ri5VBrfxC0aJQS0TR5DXHgWDurXZa7bSPx_zxRgtZbGbfY_wEeMv2PYCPR66SwNsTqPzoGLv4Z3JqYqVbUW65ibPTurje3H7pq54nWY4xds9cBQY_vCxBqCNmkX-ewrs8djNxUV56IRo_5G2YrdJ4D0D87zhzYYq1gu95kQOu0OOnCJTZcrTHoz64YLnVii0bxxqhdwKh-BgInXWlWHnVVdc_izXdXMt44e2_WPa9083f1nx1EWTm7QwGzh3tOWUykapwxqXQdH7y4huVZmGPvbawC3XQ7nYOuIim-PwqmJ7Cz6AqHtfMNolHdbFrZ-JvE4GkixR-P44IMHuJJSGF1tKXQK6GUWFvUbdm9hJUmPtwDTXvZulq_aUfZEPeESDExSq2NUpEtARrGs1HnPS8T1HPNl85V5r-z35BgMN4lDSYSYcSSCRYKegdq5mVf7bdjNOkFTwR3foZDgI8Rq-eApNSSUq0HLUPiGEu8nXBnVF5nJXmi1MkJ8KTPX8pNSpzlCz3If4SSherR2jD3-dHiFHcXU7K3WQ6u8T9bVoPrjlrUeOH-eYS_tSVwP5vUhANUU-uZ0c1yYZsZcbRpEGEgcbLOxOE-RGu2uljJek_o87ia546s9Y-1Kc3sS9BKCIdU%3D)**

clean_body: I am ready to serve Texas. Are you on my team? | It's official, Fellow Patriot. Yesterday, I filed to run for Attorney General at the Republican Party of Texas's Austin office. I could serve the 21st Congressional District of Texas for a lifetime and be professionally fulfilled. But representatives should not be permanent. It's time for a change. I am ready to come home to Texas. I want to take my experience in Congress as a federal prosecutor and First Assistant Attorney General to fight for Texas. But I cannot do this alone, Fellow Patriot. It doesn't need to be a big donation - That's why I am looking for another 28 patriots to step up from your area in the next 30 minutes. Will you be one of the folks we need? Texas is anchored by liberty, freedom, and self-determination. Let's ensure our legacy endures. Thanks, Chip Roy | | PAID FOR BY TEXANS FOR CHIP ROY Don't want to receive any more of our emails? We hate to see you go, but .

subject: End Citizens United: People, not money, decide the future

body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120947175/587871360/-2063340125?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjQwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkYWZhMjIyNS0zZWQ1LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sjsuQScYIh9U8zhxtT9IG_0PsRsKjLqBKriGlyhdtt8=)
Friend,

There are few Supreme Court cases with a larger impact on our democracy than the _Citizens United_ decision. Decided 15 years ago, it has allowed special interest groups to pour unlimited sums of money into our elections ever since.

The floodgates are open, and the consequences of corporations and billionaires using their fortunes to influence our elections is astronomical. The voices of millions of everyday Americans are being drowned out for the sake of the richest few.

**Democracy is at its best when people, not money, decide the future. That’s why I fully support legislation to overturn Citizens United and take big money out of politics -- because your vote shouldn’t have a price tag.**

As we look toward the midterm election year in just a few weeks, it’s clear how billionaires and corporations are poisoning our political process to secure their special interests while real voters and working families suffer for it.

**[I’m not standing down from this fight. If you’re with me, please sign on to my petition to pass legislation ending _Citizens United_ right now.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120947176/587871361/1547549096?refcode=em-12.9.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjQwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkYWZhMjIyNS0zZWQ1LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sjsuQScYIh9U8zhxtT9IG_0PsRsKjLqBKriGlyhdtt8=&emci=a9e9141f-3ed5-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=dafa2225-3ed5-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=13891387)**

name: Trump Enemies via Our Revolution
subject: Bombshell memo just leaked – and we’re in the crosshairs
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe
body:
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34051?t=1011&refcode=email-20251209_v&akid=9952%2E5885482%2Ev-lrDT ]Our Revolution logo

Peter Willis,

A bombshell leaked memo just directed Trump’s Department of Justice to
compile a list of "domestic terrorism" groups accused of "anti-American
sentiment." These are the tactics of dictators – and now, they’re being
aimed directly at grassroots organizations like Our Revolution.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34051?source=email-20251209&t=1060&akid=9952%2E5885482%2Ev-lrDT ]Headline: Leaked Memo shows Pam Bondi wants list of 'domestic
terrorism' groups who express 'anti-American sentiment

We’re staring down a dark turn in American history – and there is a target
directly on our back. Fox News Radio just called Our Revolution “Bernie
Sanders’ Gestapo” and we’re seeing a huge increase in attention from MAGA
mouthpieces like Breitbart aimed at us.

We refuse to be silenced, but we have to act fast and protect our
movement. We’re already investing in encrypted communications, legal
support, emergency security training, and rapid response to keep our staff
and all our activists safe – but our resources are stretched thin and our
fundraising for this month is behind.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34051?t=1057&amount=22&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&refcode=email-20251209nd&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9952%2E5885482%2Ev-lrDT ]We have always known the far-right would try to shut us down. Can you rush
a donation now to help us protect our volunteers, defend our democracy,
and fight back harder than ever?

If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your
donation to Our Revolution will process immediately.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34051?t=1042&express_lane=true&amount=9&refcode=email-20251209nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9952%2E5885482%2Ev-lrDT ]CHIP IN $9 [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34051?t=1043&express_lane=true&amount=22&refcode=email-20251209nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9952%2E5885482%2Ev-lrDT ]CHIP IN $22
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34051?t=1045&express_lane=true&amount=27&refcode=email-20251209nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9952%2E5885482%2Ev-lrDT ]CHIP IN $27 [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34051?t=1046&express_lane=true&amount=46&refcode=email-20251209nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9952%2E5885482%2Ev-lrDT ]CHIP IN $46
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34051?t=1047&express_lane=true&amount=66&refcode=email-20251209nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9952%2E5885482%2Ev-lrDT ]CHIP IN $66 [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34051?t=1048&amount=9&refcode=email-20251209nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9952%2E5885482%2Ev-lrDT ]CHIP IN OTHER AMOUNT

The new hit list of organizations could be ANY group that the Trump
administration finds to have "extreme viewpoints on immigration, radical
gender ideology, and anti-American sentiment." And once they have that
list, the memo calls on law enforcement to use Trump’s chilling National
Security directive (NSPM-7) from earlier this year to “root out all
culpable participants — including organizers and funders”.

It’s terrifying – and a clear, written plan to put groups who stand for
immigrant rights, LGBTQ+ rights, and workers’ dignity on a blacklist. They
want to turn every activist willing to speak out into a “suspect.”

That’s not America – that’s authoritarianism. And while they want to
intimidate us, together we can stand up and show them what real courage
looks like.

But the truth is: to prepare we urgently need to increase our cyber
security, grow our legal defense fund, and train our volunteers on how to
stay safe. And every dollar we spend defending ourselves is one less
dollar we can use for grassroots organizing, demonstrations, or supporting
working-class candidates running against the far right.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34051?t=1054&amount=22&refcode=email-20251209nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9952%2E5885482%2Ev-lrDT ]When the President of the United States declares all political opponents
his enemies, we have no choice but to defend ourselves. Can you chip in to
help us raise $10,000 today to hit our emergency defense fund goal and
meet this new threat head on?

When we organize, we win.

Our Revolution

Fox News Radio just called Our Revolution “Bernie Sanders’ Gestapo” and we’re seeing a huge increase in attention from MAGA mouthpieces like Breitbart aimed at us. We refuse to be silenced, but we have to act fast and protect our movement. We’re already investing in encrypted communications, legal support, emergency security training, and rapid response to keep our staff and all our activists safe – but our resources are stretched thin and our fundraising for this month is behind. [ ]We have always known the far-right would try to shut us down. Can you rush a donation now to help us protect our volunteers, defend our democracy, and fight back harder than ever? If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to Our Revolution will process immediately. [ ]CHIP IN $9 [ ]CHIP IN $22 [ ]CHIP IN $27 [ ]CHIP IN $46 [ ]CHIP IN $66 [ ]CHIP IN OTHER AMOUNT The new hit list of organizations could be ANY group that the Trump administration finds to have "extreme viewpoints on immigration, radical gender ideology, and anti-American sentiment." And once they have that list, the memo calls on law enforcement to use Trump’s chilling National Security directive (NSPM-7) from earlier this year to “root out all culpable participants — including organizers and funders”. It’s terrifying – and a clear, written plan to put groups who stand for immigrant rights, LGBTQ+ rights, and workers’ dignity on a blacklist. They want to turn every activist willing to speak out into a “suspect.” That’s not America – that’s authoritarianism. And while they want to intimidate us, together we can stand up and show them what real courage looks like. But the truth is: to prepare we urgently need to increase our cyber security, grow our legal defense fund, and train our volunteers on how to stay safe. And every dollar we spend defending ourselves is one less dollar we can use for grassroots organizing, demonstrations, or supporting working-class candidates running against the far right. [ ]When the President of the United States declares all political opponents his enemies, we have no choice but to defend ourselves. Can you chip in to help us raise $10,000 today to hit our emergency defense fund goal and meet this new threat head on? When we organize, we win. Our Revolution [ ]Facebook | [ ]Twitter | [ ]Instagram | [ ]Youtube | [ ]Website | [ ]Sign Up [ ]Our membership swag Our Revolution is the largest entirely grassroots funded progressive group in America. [ ]Help us defend democracy and take the fight directly to the establishment by committing to be a 2025 Movement Builder with a recurring donation of just $5 or more a month! name: Jasmine Crockett
subject: It’s official: I’m running for Senate.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado
body:
Together, we’re going to flip Texas blue
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001ncu8Pu91u0RFAHP30utL24MrwM7NyJTh0mrf0Wf0u6x5woAr-7znYFtO0q66-4Qr1HDAWuqiMQzuF9m7sYJOihVKSBzbry2ye6X1vE1CAo3CErQk-_aHxkCLzcsAxAODSm1mRT5fxxwLIuHhjgA4ADeu3SE7Ft0CaduvEXng6LyR7csfAq5kC2OHqjnPL90BfxEFFDtq1sqj56DtueI8dBL-7fuTFdvrnobuKIwgdXTEQ2CZK5hflA==&c=R2wj3-Hy8BldilSwkHkdmxljJZTH8raptGRaVFEdtOA_QHk1KF3qJQ==&ch=8NylAz-psHcNQWjGOY1RvQ6KOEfDwd4LrunFZlCeam8n1i0aLE3SLQ==)

|

Y'all, it's Jasmine.
I'm reaching out with big news, and I wanted you to hear it from me first: I'm officially running for the United States Senate here in Texas.
Let me be clear: With our current wannabe dictator in the White House, I'm not launching this campaign to play nice. I'm doing it to fight -- because Texas deserves someone who doesn't back down when the bullies come for her. It deserves someone who fights back.
But to win this race, we'll have to take on Donald Trump, the Texas Republican political machine, and all of their shady special interest donors. And to be blunt, that won't come cheap.
[That's why I'm asking you to become one of my founding donors as we launch this historic grassroots campaign. I'm counting on your support now more than ever before so that we can hit the ground running and finally flip Texas blue. Will you answer the call by splitting a donation between my campaign and Trisha Calvarese, Boebert's Democratic opponent?](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001ncu8Pu91u0RFAHP30utL24MrwM7NyJTh0mrf0Wf0u6x5woAr-7znYFtO0q66-4Qr1HDAWuqiMQzuF9m7sYJOihVKSBzbry2ye6X1vE1CAo3CErQk-_aHxkCLzcsAxAODSm1mRT5fxxwLIuHhjgA4ADeu3SE7Ft0CaduvEXng6LyR7csfAq5kC2OHqjnPL90BfxEFFDtq1sqj56DtueI8dBL-7fuTFdvrnobuKIwgdXTDVE34S8MxRQ==&c=R2wj3-Hy8BldilSwkHkdmxljJZTH8raptGRaVFEdtOA_QHk1KF3qJQ==&ch=8NylAz-psHcNQWjGOY1RvQ6KOEfDwd4LrunFZlCeam8n1i0aLE3SLQ==)

Let me be clear: When there's a fight worth having, I don't flinch. I don't shrink. I don't back down.
Just ask the communities I've fought for as a civil-rights attorney, the defendants I've represented as a public defender, and the people of Dallas that I serve in Congress. I'm a lifelong fighter for justice.
So Donald Trump can run his mouth all he wants. All his attacks show is that he's terrified of what I represent -- and what I'm bringing to the Senate when we win this race: truth, accountability, and a future where power answers to the people.
[If you believe that insults won't silence us -- if you believe in a Texas that stands for justice, equity, and real representation -- I need you. We're launching a campaign to change the game. But real campaigns need real support. That's why I'd be so grateful if you could pitch in $10, $25, or more to help us hit the ground running. clean_body: Together, we’re going to flip Texas blue | | | | | == | Y'all, it's Jasmine. I'm reaching out with big news, and I wanted you to hear it from me first: I'm officially running for the United States Senate here in Texas. Let me be clear: With our current wannabe dictator in the White House, I'm not launching this campaign to play nice. I'm doing it to fight -- because Texas deserves someone who doesn't back down when the bullies come for her. It deserves someone who fights back. But to win this race, we'll have to take on Donald Trump, the Texas Republican political machine, and all of their shady special interest donors. And to be blunt, that won't come cheap. == | | | == | == | == | | | == | == | == | == | Let me be clear: When there's a fight worth having, I don't flinch. I don't shrink. I don't back down. Just ask the communities I've fought for as a civil-rights attorney, the defendants I've represented as a public defender, and the people of Dallas that I serve in Congress. I'm a lifelong fighter for justice. So Donald Trump can run his mouth all he wants. All his attacks show is that he's terrified of what I represent -- and what I'm bringing to the Senate when we win this race: truth, accountability, and a future where power answers to the people. == | | | == | == | == | | | == | == | == | == | Thank you, Jasmine | | == | | | | == | Mail checks to: Trisha 4 CO 9609 S University Blvd., PO Box 631322 Highlands Ranch, CO 80163 | Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado Trisha Calvarese for Congress | 9609 S University Blvd. PO Box 631322 | Highlands Ranch, CO 80163 US | |

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120947422/587872301/-64206785?refcode=email_120925_barnes&amounts=5,15,25,50,100,250,500&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDY3MjEiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNjllMDg0M2ItM2ZkNS1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=-kOMblPlvO5jq083hD3au8_G9yVBsKWK4J5iNV3gitA=) Hi there, I wanted you to be the first to know – I’m launching my campaign to be the next governor of Wisconsin. [_**I hope you’ll take a moment to support my campaign by splitting a contribution between me and Janelle Bynum.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120947423/587872302/-64206785?refcode=email_120925_barnes&amounts=5,15,25,50,100,250,500&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDY3MjEiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNjllMDg0M2ItM2ZkNS1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=-kOMblPlvO5jq083hD3au8_G9yVBsKWK4J5iNV3gitA=) This is a critical moment for Wisconsin. **Growing up, my family’s union jobs were enough to get us a ticket to the middle-class – but today, working hard and playing by the rules no longer opens those same doors to the middle-class.** Buying a house seems impossible for too many of us. Tax loopholes continue to benefit billionaires, not middle class families. And family farmers are being squeezed by Trump’s tariffs. This moment we’re in right now demands that we step up to the plate – that we choose bold, innovative leadership that meets today’s challenges and will take on Donald Trump. I’m talking about expanding Badgercare, closing loopholes for the ultra-rich to cut taxes for middle-class working families, restoring workers’ rights, and above all, creating a better future for Wisconsinites by rejecting the Washington way and coming together behind the Wisconsin way. [_**If you’re with me, please take a moment to split a donation between my campaign and Janelle Bynum. name: Breaking
subject: Major escalation: Colorado joins redistricting fight
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fair Fight Colorado
body:
This is an existential crisis. Colorado must pick up the torch, join the fight, and meet this moment head on
| | | | | [](https://6s79z9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001XlUfE-zAOR4ndiXll1z0jfPZRvwGwi8_y8ptmRSkrSmOLTCdz14uqDq6XnV8AsRDPMVYceQibKARF4bZYL7B0s8UxCxRwSUhlvBp0OIYd7TaJ-UB5KSMwTVFJ4R9rFZaDUQ37Wx841t7aMBBRhp_KSEEjCo7fU0S2yCeSxP8bb2NP-QxCbumKwvqlPCSMKOHk-OFJU9qEr-QGu13GrW5dYRDt975ZRSI0O1WmAIV9Y0=&c=e8RUNfEoVlqNi3DG-J66T6J4XWPXsBQCAxBjpluv7uI91VVYacDKPA==&ch=_G1g-ryuEn6V3aaJGc9mMx3-7r994podDG0Xf54hW8CjQYi4kr4x4g==)
---
Peter,
If Democrats reclaim the House next November the MAGA agenda hits a brick wall. Trump will become a lame duck president incapable of fulfilling any further policy goals. And right now a blue wave and a cataclysmic GOP defeat is exactly what we're on track for.
The only way MAGA can survive 2026 is if they rig the game.
Which is why in red states across the country, Trump is pushing for new congressional maps that will make up for the dozens of seats they are set to lose. A move that could establish one party rule for generations.
---
| [](https://6s79z9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001XlUfE-zAOR4ndiXll1z0jfPZRvwGwi8_y8ptmRSkrSmOLTCdz14uqDq6XnV8AsRDazZEq3TCsTB_td8nIh5u-Nmsxmah4Uiqlkjo88OuSSE2RJWg3Gm6lHFe1PdB2a4PI5Gkxxf7FIiElskliIxRrV1tZ6inCHUWwBzwPFPrk8viFAnEdX4ppZIfPQMfgcX1rCsEkiDR407GN-3AhRYPBoB3nBAfxsNHW_Zhj9Yi1K0=&c=e8RUNfEoVlqNi3DG-J66T6J4XWPXsBQCAxBjpluv7uI91VVYacDKPA==&ch=_G1g-ryuEn6V3aaJGc9mMx3-7r994podDG0Xf54hW8CjQYi4kr4x4g==)
---
The GOP is moving fast. Gerrymandered maps have passed in Texas, North Carolina, Missouri, and a push for redistricting is underway in Indiana and Florida with more red states soon to follow.
This is an existential crisis. No matter how unpopular Trump becomes, if left unchecked, MAGA will create enough new red seats to ensure their House majority is insurmountable making it impossible for Dems to reclaim the House.
However, Americans are pushing back. Last month, California fired the opening blow of the Democratic counter-salvo with their vote to pass Proposition 50, a ballot measure that gives CA a fair congressional map to counter Trump's gerrymandering push.
But California alone won't be enough. To save our Democracy, blue states like Colorado must pick up the torch, join the fight, and meet this moment head on
This is why Fair Fight Colorado is proud to announce that we are introducing the ballot measure that will give every Coloradan a chance to vote on a new Congressional map that will make Colorado bluer.
We have the power to level the playing field and counter Trump's power grab with fair maps that will put power back in the hands of the people but it will require an astronomically expensive push to get this on the ballot and in front of Colorado voters.
MAGA is about to throw everything they've got into stopping us and we need to brace for impact. [Will you rush a contribution of $50, $25, or even $5, to become a founding donor and help Colorado fight fire with fire?](https://6s79z9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001XlUfE-zAOR4ndiXll1z0jfPZRvwGwi8_y8ptmRSkrSmOLTCdz14uqDq6XnV8AsRDQAVnwEtOOK0bCZG1U64eKcfVuzEjlMEWJ6djS4vlsQYGYuEH9XVagH_8MC-K823jrgklAU5-hss9N3JkqcOrCmvCBFS_kdfO0_XJrWByA-8cuMOMUYL0HY9VJt6Qj4_P4e0Sj74YjgsktB3XfS2ePZW1S0VxruSDpFAON_9tB_SiUwEdY3AP5w==&c=e8RUNfEoVlqNi3DG-J66T6J4XWPXsBQCAxBjpluv7uI91VVYacDKPA==&ch=_G1g-ryuEn6V3aaJGc9mMx3-7r994podDG0Xf54hW8CjQYi4kr4x4g==)

clean_body: This is an existential crisis. Colorado must pick up the torch, join the fight, and meet this moment head on | | | | | == Peter, If Democrats reclaim the House next November the MAGA agenda hits a brick wall. Trump will become a lame duck president incapable of fulfilling any further policy goals. And right now a blue wave and a cataclysmic GOP defeat is exactly what we're on track for. The only way MAGA can survive 2026 is if they rig the game. Which is why in red states across the country, Trump is pushing for new congressional maps that will make up for the dozens of seats they are set to lose. A move that could establish one party rule for generations. | == The GOP is moving fast. Gerrymandered maps have passed in Texas, North Carolina, Missouri, and a push for redistricting is underway in Indiana and Florida with more red states soon to follow. This is an existential crisis. No matter how unpopular Trump becomes, if left unchecked, MAGA will create enough new red seats to ensure their House majority is insurmountable making it impossible for Dems to reclaim the House. However, Americans are pushing back. Last month, California fired the opening blow of the Democratic counter-salvo with their vote to pass Proposition 50, a ballot measure that gives CA a fair congressional map to counter Trump's gerrymandering push. But California alone won't be enough. To save our Democracy, blue states like Colorado must pick up the torch, join the fight, and meet this moment head on This is why Fair Fight Colorado is proud to announce that we are introducing the ballot measure that will give every Coloradan a chance to vote on a new Congressional map that will make Colorado bluer. We have the power to level the playing field and counter Trump's power grab with fair maps that will put power back in the hands of the people but it will require an astronomically expensive push to get this on the ballot and in front of Colorado voters. MAGA is about to throw everything they've got into stopping us and we need to brace for impact. == If you've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly: | | == | == | == | | == | == | == | == Together we can fight back, make history, and ensure that Colorado plays its part in ensuring a fair election. Thank you for standing with us. Val Nosler Beck Executive Director Fair Fight Colorado | | | | == | | == | Donate by mail: Fair Fight Colorado == == | Paid for by Fair Fight Colorado. Registered agent: David Scott Martinez. | | | | | | Fair Fight Colorado | 1601 Arapahoe Street FL 10 | Denver, CO 80202 US | |

name: Kerry McQuisten (Newsletter)
subject: Merry Christmas - What a Year!
body:
| | | | |
---
Hello, Oregon... and beyond! I'm starting this newsletter with some great news. As a new organization, Oregon Conservative Caucus started our process of flipping Oregon red with Tillamook County throughout 2025. We've chiseled away at that county and are within about a dozen voters of flipping it back from blue! If you'd like to help us as we finish flipping Tillamook and move on to other counties, go here: [Oregon Conservative Caucus](https://sptr.eocampaign1.com/f/a/RHDQbHx_Tjsv1WMogxUokw~~/AAAHURA~/S5GMCrnCVPZNJ1CWe1VrEc8MG81vAZiWCizr8hvxb8P8TL9_51fii5K9S5bblBw-rYRJE7htdZ5fbZt11jPHonYsWkAz0UpN8uvHt1W1l8KCvKapxQDbf0cbQdfKiGYfYVpJDsStlbENN5jywpP_mnYvvScViT_tnjzbWi5Eq6EiH2hxBTji3QCM6Je4ZnLC). We're 100% volunteer so donations and your time are needed. I personally want to tackle Deschutes...
_Okay... on to the festivities!_
Back in November, Solomon and I had a call from the White House saying, "Hey! Any interest in looking at the decorations?" We jumped at the chance. We then had another surprise call - they'd "pulled some strings" and seated us behind the cabinet members and their families right in front of the President's box at the National Christmas Tree Lighting. So, off we went.
I'm told about 17,000 people filled the Ellipse and surrounding park area to watch the lighting, but I'm not sure. Media and security told us the turnout was a vast improvement over Biden's last lighting. He'd kept the audience waiting from 3-7 p.m. for him to show up in the freezing cold, so most of the audience walked away before he arrived. That meant producers scrambled to fill the seats by the cameras. I do know we also froze. We were layered up and had Little Hotties, but they didn't work. Solomon climbed up into one of the TV camera nests to get a wind break and some different video footage, and made friends with the photographers! I was able to watch President Trump load up in front of the White House from my seat, then saw the motorcade swing around behind the unlit tree. I knew he and Melania were ready to walk on stage when Susie Wiles ducked into the audience. They stayed behind the bullet-proof glass. FLOTUS hit the button. POTUS spoke. Then thousands of people got up at once and made a rush for the street. It was a zoo! That meant the entire concert lineup happened after most of us left. They aired the entire ceremony on TV the next day, but it wouldn't look like it did in person. Imagine lengthy pauses for production changes, reels of last year's performances on screens to keep us occupied, announcements about how to flee the site in case of an emergency...
The next day we wandered around the White House, and it looked like a winter wonderland on the first day of snow in D.C. this year. We had a lot of time to walk through unrushed as they were casually setting up for Bocelli to come sing that evening. A Secret Service agent or two were stationed in each room to keep an eye on things. Solomon has been to more receptions, dinners, Christmas parties and meetings there than he can count, so none of this was new to him; it was new to me and my daughter, Vivien, though. The White House is not _that_ big compared to other buildings, and the design is much simpler than that of the capitol buildings in many other countries. But the history there? _That's_ what fascinates. We could peek through the barricade wall to see the construction of the new ballroom. I do think the new design will be a vast improvement over the former East Wing. The space and the upgrades are needed.
We toured the museum at Ford's Theatre, saw President Lincoln's murder weapon, and watched "A Christmas Carol." We went up to the top of the Washington Monument, took Vivien to the Lincoln Memorial, and walked along the reflecting pool. (Me: Jenny! Vivien: Forrest!)
We also hit the State Department for a couple meetings with senior-level officials and attorneys regarding Republicans Overseas and also the project we're working on to help remove CCP-tied visa-holders who come into the U.S. inciting violence or simply seeking asylum on false pretenses. This project is right up Solomon's alley. The State Department is the most interesting to me of all the departments in the administration, hands down.
A great ending to a great year. And with that, I wish you all a very, very MERRY CHRISTMAS and a happy, healthy New Year.
_Kerry_
PS - I noticed a bunch of you added at once to this newsletter. That usually means a friend of yours wanted to add a group. If you've received this against your will, just quietly hit "unsubscribe" at the bottom. No harm, no foul. Otherwise, stick around!

clean_body: | | | | | Hello, Oregon... and beyond! I'm starting this newsletter with some great news. As a new organization, Oregon Conservative Caucus started our process of flipping Oregon red with Tillamook County throughout 2025. We've chiseled away at that county and are within about a dozen voters of flipping it back from blue! If you'd like to help us as we finish flipping Tillamook and move on to other counties, go here: . We're 100% volunteer so donations and your time are needed. I personally want to tackle Deschutes... Okay... on to the festivities! Back in November, Solomon and I had a call from the White House saying, "Hey! Any interest in looking at the decorations?" We jumped at the chance. We then had another surprise call - they'd "pulled some strings" and seated us behind the cabinet members and their families right in front of the President's box at the National Christmas Tree Lighting. So, off we went. I'm told about 17,000 people filled the Ellipse and surrounding park area to watch the lighting, but I'm not sure. Media and security told us the turnout was a vast improvement over Biden's last lighting. He'd kept the audience waiting from 3-7 p.m. for him to show up in the freezing cold, so most of the audience walked away before he arrived. That meant producers scrambled to fill the seats by the cameras. I do know we also froze. We were layered up and had Little Hotties, but they didn't work. Solomon climbed up into one of the TV camera nests to get a wind break and some different video footage, and made friends with the photographers! I was able to watch President Trump load up in front of the White House from my seat, then saw the motorcade swing around behind the unlit tree. I knew he and Melania were ready to walk on stage when Susie Wiles ducked into the audience. They stayed behind the bullet-proof glass. FLOTUS hit the button. POTUS spoke. Then thousands of people got up at once and made a rush for the street. It was a zoo! That meant the entire concert lineup happened after most of us left. They aired the entire ceremony on TV the next day, but it wouldn't look like it did in person. Imagine lengthy pauses for production changes, reels of last year's performances on screens to keep us occupied, announcements about how to flee the site in case of an emergency... The next day we wandered around the White House, and it looked like a winter wonderland on the first day of snow in D.C. this year. We had a lot of time to walk through unrushed as they were casually setting up for Bocelli to come sing that evening. A Secret Service agent or two were stationed in each room to keep an eye on things. Solomon has been to more receptions, dinners, Christmas parties and meetings there than he can count, so none of this was new to him; it was new to me and my daughter, Vivien, though. The White House is not that big compared to other buildings, and the design is much simpler than that of the capitol buildings in many other countries. But the history there? That's what fascinates. We could peek through the barricade wall to see the construction of the new ballroom. I do think the new design will be a vast improvement over the former East Wing. The space and the upgrades are needed. We toured the museum at Ford's Theatre, saw President Lincoln's murder weapon, and watched "A Christmas Carol." We went up to the top of the Washington Monument, took Vivien to the Lincoln Memorial, and walked along the reflecting pool. (Me: Jenny! Vivien: Forrest!) We also hit the State Department for a couple meetings with senior-level officials and attorneys regarding Republicans Overseas and also the project we're working on to help remove CCP-tied visa-holders who come into the U.S. inciting violence or simply seeking asylum on false pretenses. This project is right up Solomon's alley. The State Department is the most interesting to me of all the departments in the administration, hands down. A great ending to a great year. And with that, I wish you all a very, very MERRY CHRISTMAS and a happy, healthy New Year. Kerry PS - I noticed a bunch of you added at once to this newsletter. That usually means a friend of yours wanted to add a group. If you've received this against your will, just quietly hit "unsubscribe" at the bottom. No harm, no foul. Otherwise, stick around! | | Past Newsletters ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ * ~ | | The podcast is picking up listeners and advertisers! You can listen you'd like. | | | ~ ---|--- | ~ "KerryMcQuisten.com" You received this email because you subscribed to our list. If a friend or family member subscribed for you and you don't wish to receive these newsletters, you can: ~~ at any time. name: Mark Kelly
subject: My decision
body:
We’ve been in some tough fights before, but this one is different. The President of the United States threatened my life, personally. I’ve got the entire executive branch bearing down on me right now. |
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120948146/587875499/148511826?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDE1MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2OWEyOGIwNS00MmQ1LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=82oGUIti-Bq71cc0YoI7Y4nwJQ8MDm1hHgSYssG0JcY=)
Hey Peter, it’s Mark Kelly.

Seven years ago, I was making a decision about whether I was going to run for the Senate.

I knew it would be a lot of work. A lot of time away from home. A lot of time away from family. I knew it wouldn’t be easy.

But after two years of Donald Trump and a Republican-held Senate doing just about everything he wanted, I knew I couldn’t sit on the sidelines. I had to do something.

So, I ran. And, somehow, we won. And then we had to turn around and run all over again to win a full term after that. But thanks to your support, we won that one, too.

I’m grateful every day that I get to wake up, walk out the door, and defend our people, our democracy, and our Constitution. It’s one of the greatest honors of my life. And I’ve never forgotten that I wouldn’t be here without your help.

We’ve been in some tough fights before, but this one is different. The President of the United States threatened my life, personally. I’ve got the entire executive branch bearing down on me right now.

After everything you’ve already done for me, I shouldn’t have to ask you for more. But that’s what I need to do.

**[I’m asking you to split a donation between George Whitesides and me, and I hope you know that I wouldn’t be asking if this wasn’t important.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120948147/587875500/-156400434?refcode=em-12.9.25-all&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDE1MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2OWEyOGIwNS00MmQ1LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=82oGUIti-Bq71cc0YoI7Y4nwJQ8MDm1hHgSYssG0JcY=)**

clean_body: We’ve been in some tough fights before, but this one is different. The President of the United States threatened my life, personally. I’ve got the entire executive branch bearing down on me right now. | = Hey Peter, it’s Mark Kelly. Seven years ago, I was making a decision about whether I was going to run for the Senate. I knew it would be a lot of work. A lot of time away from home. A lot of time away from family. I knew it wouldn’t be easy. But after two years of Donald Trump and a Republican-held Senate doing just about everything he wanted, I knew I couldn’t sit on the sidelines. I had to do something. So, I ran. And, somehow, we won. And then we had to turn around and run all over again to win a full term after that. But thanks to your support, we won that one, too. I’m grateful every day that I get to wake up, walk out the door, and defend our people, our democracy, and our Constitution. It’s one of the greatest honors of my life. And I’ve never forgotten that I wouldn’t be here without your help. We’ve been in some tough fights before, but this one is different. The President of the United States threatened my life, personally. I’ve got the entire executive branch bearing down on me right now. After everything you’ve already done for me, I shouldn’t have to ask you for more. But that’s what I need to do. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | **= | = ---|--- = | = = | =** Thank you. Mark | = = = George has spent his career solving problems. Minnesota is a national leader in democracy.
* We have one of the**highest voter turnout rates** in America.
* We passed **automatic voter registration** and **restored the freedom to vote** for thousands of returning citizens.
* We **strengthened access** for tribal nations and created pre-registration for students.

**But this progress is under threat.**
* The Trump Administration has already threatened to ban voting by mail.
* They demanded private data for millions of Minnesota voters.
* They weakened the federal agencies that help protect election security.
* And they have refused to reassure us that they will stay out of state-run elections in 2026.

**These are not hypothetical risks. They are happening now.**
Here in Minnesota, we have pushed back every step of the way. We sued the administration and won. We refused to turn over private voter data. And we are preparing for every scenario to keep our election fair, accurate, honest, and secure.
**But I cannot do this alone. Not in this moment.**
**[If you believe in protecting the freedom to vote, if you believe in truth over disinformation, if you believe Minnesota must remain a national leader in democracy, I’m asking you personally: Will you become a Founding Member today and help us launch the strongest pro-democracy campaign in the country?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120948381/587876287/329411655?amount=25&refcode=ss_em_fr_IA_251209&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS8xMDAzNTUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOWNjMGE5NDgtNDJkNS1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=rLNCvHQ0JlHYUzB7QUDDvZPsSZwSMtOuV4rffDIY_1g=)**

**Fewer than 120 spots are still open, and they’re going quickly. You have been on my radar all year for my official nice list! I don’t want you to miss out!**

The season of giving is upon us, and just one more gift of $47 today will launch you to the TOP of my Official Nice List!

This will sound oddly familiar...but it’s what I’m doing: **I'm making a list and checking it twice.**
Don’t be naughty please, be nice!

Donald J. Trump

body:
✊ Power exists inside all of us. Display it with our FREE PBTP stickers! | | | Hey Peter, time’s running out to order your FREE sticker sheet! ⏰ Thank you for building power alongside us throughout 2025! In a moment of adversity, our community’s courage and resilience did not go unnoticed. As a small token of our appreciation, we dropped an exclusive batch of “Powered by the People” sticker sheets! Each sticker carries an impactful reminder to continue building power together in 2026. 🧡 **[Can we send you a FREE sticker set to carry this message everywhere you go?](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm39v1dMWTA_hIjJmaJKEvPG6O32K0h6_HfwaPTAVslGPNyjwjROlo43Td_HHT1gBTwS1fkgE4__jjvhz0dobmgkWIjkDhKMqqp3flk0HRG19EGC46KuPemP_sJaBWfIM68lLNQLqcFWuXx86B8E5nPYmRJOXD-6Kjfgk6iD0pnBRlnJTgDgtCQjFHel6sMTQ_Wyaee4jd-dlkZ83bKTAWhRK_4SmZy2TGwEZ6n1C5Z38tW8VJiTC2XfdrN_OnyFC_2ZyJvMNOFrroONka18D8hhvwisYzJbqlhnQEdCTf4zVSaow1cvIUAwbFaLj9jIiYhlxu8n79_1iD2g_I5EnHLuw/4ma/Z9jdBjbTReeNh6VuFMdojA/h0/h001.zVVZX989NOxINxI1WGePkDuExEDs4cd4IrvQfVcH2hM)** **[Order your FREE stickers!](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm39v1dMWTA_hIjJmaJKEvPG6O32K0h6_HfwaPTAVslGPNyjwjROlo43Td_HHT1gBTwS1fkgE4__jjvhz0dobmgkWIjkDhKMqqp3flk0HRG19EFpOMGivWkWMxx5QKob_3v_p4jXbYLzx55k-bYWG1-MMVKFQgBaBY9MgjquVg2ur4JrsNh334Dp3NgwbM5oiRR3dzbaaNi0rAUQS0qAp0zdAeWXGjB0vgXEcVYGQuXP_oe-V7IYFoaSqg8cSYPOnVDB39gQigSPXZTGN1iJ6UfDyKsWs-kEBYk7BDZDDY1s07ebjPYHdKM_SNbauwg4NEweyYfASflfAZ01PCkn6NL7l/4ma/Z9jdBjbTReeNh6VuFMdojA/h1/h001.hhvQTN9eDiXRZUJffxsWA8OO2rrSnaPjWNi6Ln7Ub_s)** **** [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm39v1dMWTA_hIjJmaJKEvPG6O32K0h6_HfwaPTAVslGPNyjwjROlo43Td_HHT1gBTwS1fkgE4__jjvhz0dobmgkWIjkDhKMqqp3flk0HRG19EMuNLbdR-MC7DuK_1896h4IZ8ht8e_aZ2WLE1--EdLCQum9Mf9sAQ4Pe4P7cT9sW1qZ-H-eMaRACe3P1pyiLO0it1ALIQUqm50hS8-oavvgKSiZqCt1OqC0bNWEPcA_Af2FAfbfn8lo5MJXPbGrd3Of-n_iVJ11fxdtYi7aESiNoYigN6TgztOKieabcBmr40tIzAJ-P8WSG8T47XUg6j1NJRHiqOty3yxRkM04jbNlR/4ma/Z9jdBjbTReeNh6VuFMdojA/h2/h001.GHhRifeC2jLLs44ZsyynZ0tKIQvHov8do1jnvaYHRMA)
This year, you brought our Powered by the People campaign to life, delivering a clear message to working-people everywhere of the future we are capable of building when we stand united. Together, we:
* 💌 Delivered notes of solidarity to our neighbors across the country
* 🍧 Spread love (and poetry) to teachers, street vendors, and working-class families who power our communities everyday
* 🔥 Gathered at our FIRST ever Digital Congress to build community and reconnect with movement elders who guide us on our path to liberation

**And, throughout it all, we found big and small ways to gather with our friends and neighbors, remembering that our power comes from one another.** From attending trainings to sharpen our organizing skills, to hosting grounding events for our community, we showed up with care and love for one another. clean_body: ✊ Power exists inside all of us. Display it with our FREE PBTP stickers! | | | Hey Peter, time’s running out to order your FREE sticker sheet! ⏰ Thank you for building power alongside us throughout 2025! In a moment of adversity, our community’s courage and resilience did not go unnoticed. As a small token of our appreciation, we dropped an exclusive batch of “Powered by the People” sticker sheets! Each sticker carries an impactful reminder to continue building power together in 2026. 🧡 ** This year, you brought our Powered by the People campaign to life, delivering a clear message to working-people everywhere of the future we are capable of building when we stand united. Together, we: 💌 Delivered notes of solidarity to our neighbors across the country 🍧 Spread love (and poetry) to teachers, street vendors, and working-class families who power our communities everyday 🔥 Gathered at our FIRST ever Digital Congress to build community and reconnect with movement elders who guide us on our path to liberation And, throughout it all, we found big and small ways to gather with our friends and neighbors, remembering that our power comes from one another. From attending trainings to sharpen our organizing skills, to hosting grounding events for our community, we showed up with care and love for one another. 🫶 It’ll take all of us to build the world we’ve always dreamed of. Can you invite your friends to claim their free sticker & commit to building the future we need? In community, Ximena and the UWD team | Follow us on: | | | | |

name: Vivek Ramaswamy
subject: From the bottom of my heart
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VIVEK RAMASWAMY FOR OHIO
body:
I will never take your sacrifice for granted.
[](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=86d7015890c2455c9ed191ded83a3dc9&_e=ssZiXgAy_8l2b-KExdAujGN8nBMWJO-HOJrBthzODhDZEzJZBvpYqIxPVKO7YZc8ij8eODtdICQHBfn9J9yL9ty8WP_HgiL92SsmkVSoIRT-yulUZPW2ocXPjze9c9lXdtNYgZoV-VG7ffD9x8ClkcdhRJNb8u6Q_U39rgsnpN1w5yN0nZSX1yr9kvN5hK7expCWpruiPpMx0uc8p2jSWSi1w08-kpKpLe00FRfhpUEOcDUHwxWq_VIJY9hIOBKH0rTPCBDwPXgXTBxoidBWyqcmXp_nXzs_P8Q2Hqz_Hr5i1rtFUzx-zyrk5KuJCWHxlbYIdg90UERNSDEsOrPqlF9stJaOWlCrB6Lf9qI6Fcra6-0VbWKXOD40Nr8Gf-Z87LMgqdrnAz6aSS3dI3AXCRE_gYrcBOJn6p893j150Bzor3bZCdyjBr87cTc6N-5KKkoa3VS8FcqG5KcVry25Eyw79iu7tdJN_NHxArkjKEHjsOdj9sN9VC9sKsgaVcKhiJpnlYN8iD-nJYfFXMwBbJWThb6XywPGCYSr8mXSCQgz_LCNKOU4b94d2yQdqAKq7FHwat6OgRXvu2wdGYbDKewpSEFGtUfe-jdvT87JR_gbqAd0nnL3XVhZXhkb1D6Mixw42n7yYX1esFhZMUNzJ92P26aGTU4xcep-X7ImzZyARZP39V0Rkd2GT9ItcH7NoORh0bGmwn6LdP8bFC7iFPpVudLw8aol16Uv84NaQD-YOS6Odl1NkGCr6HEDSPHk8tk6U3p0_VsAFmj6PAYLMqmVAcd4leybbSXZkHPtIf4s-GN7m1rWe4285tYGELLVZ1OPSyGIBI8yqFQ1BXHQLsvQJ23XJhPewxE12V_6WE6rYsTu-WpVNEwi_NLy82EpLPwGH999ensn21YUCRgXYl5Z_q7ZAG05WMmY5giMf-c%3D)Politics today is full of noise - phony deadlines, scare tactics, and cheap gimmicks.
But I want to cut through the noise for a moment.
[I’d like to tell you a quick story.](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=86d7015890c2455c9ed191ded83a3dc9&_e=ssZiXgAy_8l2b-KExdAujGN8nBMWJO-HOJrBthzODhDZEzJZBvpYqIxPVKO7YZc8ij8eODtdICQHBfn9J9yL9ty8WP_HgiL92SsmkVSoIRT-yulUZPW2ocXPjze9c9lXdtNYgZoV-VG7ffD9x8ClkcdhRJNb8u6Q_U39rgsnpN1w5yN0nZSX1yr9kvN5hK7expCWpruiPpMx0uc8p2jSWSi1w08-kpKpLe00FRfhpUEOcDUHwxWq_VIJY9hIOBKH0rTPCBDwPXgXTBxoidBWyqcmXp_nXzs_P8Q2Hqz_Hr5i1rtFUzx-zyrk5KuJCWHxlbYIdg90UERNSDEsOrPqlF9stJaOWlCrB6Lf9qI6FcrcezZKw4Iu_Y8nRtYJPXR61yppNvuNSUGH7J9cI2Ed4vuaL6SFSIgEUBP6-zwJlX1TVGNWw_jy7pixYOYHmlx0V80TP414-2xmPK-DjwI9nrWyyNmhL_vsHNmH4D6AX8Yg9JVKzFw0wJxMUFLdf8pjVvYjF20Uzgixxib2SNwQGNi82Ac7EOiEppTmbsYKIIusX-Kou9Vs0IuZICMlUEZ3pHxFTn6YunQVpUAeqQP-kdKKgAi_4x3uyhdgtVmyQmLKz6OemoBXqcEgmV_cbgWMuAYbJa6W554iAF_bh3PFFfS7jIXWHUrojdS6Y4Sikx4KoSrGjCN1Up4Oa40R-IYe3vMsV5phfOk_PegTyUwyf979djobkPVP1hrX-JpEjAriJ229lAhg-nKnuuC_mUMQeka99yEphGIml-xkcntaaNMrouji3vvAYGP3-wLUFh4UkifRujtyYCTYxG0jJDdtn7C4I8A-F8tRAq4eCfNqYUKyxzF6ki_S05E5uavEcxq5Y08gY1Mx0QLExrRhhQTRJ4_rOixQm6OgxW3K-OX6Fh_EX_OG_gROerLyDZMWDMo%3D)
I grew up right here in Ohio. My parents worked hard to give me a chance at the American Dream, and **I know what sacrifice looks like**. Every opportunity I've had was built on the back of that sacrifice.[](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=86d7015890c2455c9ed191ded83a3dc9&_e=ssZiXgAy_8l2b-KExdAujGN8nBMWJO-HOJrBthzODhDZEzJZBvpYqIxPVKO7YZc8ij8eODtdICQHBfn9J9yL9ty8WP_HgiL92SsmkVSoIRT-yulUZPW2ocXPjze9c9lXdtNYgZoV-VG7ffD9x8ClkcdhRJNb8u6Q_U39rgsnpN1w5yN0nZSX1yr9kvN5hK7expCWpruiPpMx0uc8p2jSWSi1w08-kpKpLe00FRfhpUEOcDUHwxWq_VIJY9hIOBKH0rTPCBDwPXgXTBxoidBWyqcmXp_nXzs_P8Q2Hqz_Hr5i1rtFUzx-zyrk5KuJCWHxlbYIdg90UERNSDEsOrPqlF9stJaOWlCrB6Lf9qI6Fcp4IQERT0xTpd0PJd2ZHDZFQllhCQ5OffhOb-gF4kQuT7ACbcV3f9RMr6Mz8Fad0VQl6kpCo_joPzCfllbIecIDgQSifdO4X8x9XNpb1NZqMCMgfB-hOWbEpUU3mePs1SwSUMIALVEax18MYZju6WsKHCSXY3GLkLnQduMnueGv0zWe33ndi8Sc4-jPQaKTil08V4ewTQ21pyFagaxKob8iXQMbKQOa_6UDIUZEHNVf_EQjKVw_yJpEmi4jrNnrnSMdouDSfgkpgwBMrXq33JzCRuCuRiygMVU2G2YnuFq6uLRP2uadcjQxM-uM4iQigG9n0PRruwJOfiPCBUwxu7Imy8AC9Rh4PWuuvtwMiko2rMXxofe4x8MAbnpkgXUdYkLTVM7dY5o0_1aUdM-MJa_gt7XiaoFZgux5i4pAS5UO9JVtQh_Miamj9RBfMDbFwVObJA5PREpJcfxJttYgITtZA64z6jhRBvtvQEW9mIQuQrTqpT82q1_p1-ocK34JvyCG768cSMvRZNUdUlEH0JCoy7gZRE3tOcr5Osigv-UkaQT511HQheJgkyP05z_0ils%3D)That's why I never forget what it means when someone like you chips in a few dollars. It isn't "just money." **
**It 's trust. It's belief. It's a vote of confidence in the movement we're building in Ohio and beyond. **
**So whether you 've already donated or you're considering it today, let me say this clearly: **

clean_body: I will never take your sacrifice for granted. today is full of noise - phony deadlines, scare tactics, and cheap gimmicks. But I want to cut through the noise for a moment. I grew up right here in Ohio. My parents worked hard to give me a chance at the American Dream, and I know what sacrifice looks like. Every opportunity I've had was built on the back of that sacrifice. why I never forget what it means when someone like you chips in a few dollars. It isn't "just money." It 's trust. It's belief. It's a vote of confidence in the movement we're building in Ohio and beyond. So whether you 've already donated or you're considering it today, let me say this clearly: We 're in an extremely tough fight. And this isn't just any race - it's shaping up to be one of the most important in the country. I threw my hat in the ring now because we have a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to save our country. I mean that. We were from a country you and I wouldn't recognize anymore. President Trump saved us - with the help of folks like you - from that dire outcome. With that said, he needs loyal partners at the state level to help him succeed. That 's why this race means so much. So if you 've given before (even just once, even just a buck) know this: you've already made a difference, and I will never take your sacrifice for granted. And if you haven 't given yet - - I'd be honored to have you on my team. Every single gift, no matter the size, fuels this must-win fight. grateful,Vivek Ramaswamy the election were held today, we 'd come up just short.We can't afford to lose this ground -- not now, not after everything we've built.Your support right now makes the difference between a comeback and a collapse.**| | | | PAID FOR BY VIVEK RAMASWAMY FOR OHIO 350 E 1st Ave Ste 100 Columbus, OH 43201
