name: Jenny Beth Martin, Tea Party Patriots Action
subject: Call To Action: Democrats Do You Stand with Our Military or Narcoterrorists?
body:
Stand with President Trump and our military stopping drugs from entering our country
[](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.teapartypatriots.org/1/0100019b04f94241-aa370dba-8ac0-454e-8a04-b14286cc63a5-000000/iUcpmfHKeLIUmMkiwLPQLuOVjRi6tOB86YS47jig6W8=435)Patriot–Narco-terrorists are flooding our streets with illegal drugs. These narco-terrorists, designated as a Foreign Terrorist Organization (FTO), are **DIRECTLY responsible for the deaths of HUNDREDS OF THOUSANDS of Americans**.President Trump and our military are fighting back!On President Trump’s orders, U.S. forces are taking out narco-terrorists in international waters as they haul their poison toward our shores.Recently, six Democrats released a video urging U.S. troops to defy “manifestly unlawful” orders. Democrats have sunk to a new low, openly encouraging troops to defy the chain of command, not follow lawful orders, and undermine President Trump’s authority.[_**Call Congress and demand that they lock arms with President Trump**_](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpatriotproject.sitecenter.us%2Fpetition%2Fc-i-c%2F/1/0100019b04f94241-aa370dba-8ac0-454e-8a04-b14286cc63a5-000000/K0Mb0dC1QRjY0FMEFU85ffjWxQLDexssppAPjBWOplY=435) and our military to stop the flood of drugs into our country.[TELL CONGRESS: STOP THE NARCOS](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpatriotproject.sitecenter.us%2Fpetition%2Fc-i-c%2F/2/0100019b04f94241-aa370dba-8ac0-454e-8a04-b14286cc63a5-000000/gEHJ92zntpzdm5a0g__EYp4nOXo-XYd0cC0C_dntF1Y=435)In liberty,Jenny Beth Martin[CONTRIBUTE](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.teapartypatriots.org%2Fdonate%2F/1/0100019b04f94241-aa370dba-8ac0-454e-8a04-b14286cc63a5-000000/Zl2mIQ4M1YKJgg10iQC54Wl-Uwmr0wfvNhXmt7T6LLA=435) _This email list is a joint venture of the following separate organizations: Tea Party Patriots Action, Inc., Tea Party Patriots Foundation, and Tea Party Patriots Citizens Fund. The outrage over the Trump-Hegseth drone strikes in the Caribbean is all thoroughly appropriate. They amount to war crimes. Full stop.
And, also....
[F]ar from being in fact targeted, Obama's orders amounted to death sentences for people--particularly in Afghanistan--effectively chosen at random.
....
Today, Trump & Hegseth deploy the Pentagon to commit the very same crimes.
### **" Thou Shalt Not Kill" **
Nothing in the Constitution empowers the President to unilaterally start wars, or kill people without due process. In fact, the document might be understood as an elaborate scheme [precisely](https://substack.com/redirect/3686e860-3e25-4a50-898f-58d82b8ab217?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to limit executive power. Few goals would make more sense for the founders of a nascent Republic shrugging off the rule of an imperial monarch.
Setting aside the Constitution to instead examine the other text which our leaders claim to follow, we find an even more direct prohibition in the Bible on what we've all witnessed in the Gulf of ([ahem](https://substack.com/redirect/6e043a67-beaa-43c2-9e2e-236e77e375be?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)) Mexico.
As many have suggested, the Pentagon's strikes in the Caribbean have been tantamount to [murder](https://substack.com/redirect/b8bc8c6f-f596-4fdf-b7d0-d9002b19c130?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Several angles, however, have appeared to remain widely unnoticed in the public conversation.
_Thanks for reading Chronicles of a Dying Empire! You can help inform & inspire your friends by sharing this post._
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=181167648&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgxMTY3NjQ4LCJpYXQiOjE3NjUzMTU0NjYsImV4cCI6MTc2NzkwNzQ2NiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.ewn0EARec1_AcFSdX_k3PfRknzQlYffAMQIpg7X4-2I)
### They know not what they do
Beyond constitutional limits on executive power, international human rights principles--which America once [fought and won](https://substack.com/redirect/19668fd9-a597-4602-b13a-f8d3f2a4202c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) a World War to initially establish--also require a charge and trial before any use of lethal force by government actors.
Requirements to respect universal human rights grow doubly important when states act across borders. The original purpose of the United Nations--which America also played a crucial role in establishing after the Second World War--was to help prevent another one from ever breaking out.
How quickly our country has forgotten lessons won at the cost of so much blood!
### **Race, dissent, and military ethics**
Many have voiced outrage over the seemingly forced [resignation](https://substack.com/redirect/0d20ba7c-4877-4bad-94ac-bfec2451c9fb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of Admiral Alvin Holsey, a senior judge advocate general (JAG) who had raised thoughtful, important--and internationally mandated--concerns that lethal strikes ordered by Secretary of War Pete Hegseth may have been (and in fact absolutely were) akin to murder.
Holsey is a senior officer who chose to fall on his sword rather than follow illegal orders. His resignation reflects a [unique conundrum](https://substack.com/redirect/ba31791a-7161-462f-8c94-bb9f3aa07a08?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) confronting military servicemembers, who face an international legal obligation to respect human rights, on the one hand, and conflicting courts-martial if they take action on that obligation by refusing the follow the chain of command.
That conundrum should make the sacrifices of heroes like [Aaron Bushnell](https://substack.com/redirect/94ac34d2-70b3-4ef9-bfea-79ac62a00803?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (or, for that matter, Edward Snowden or [Daniel Ellsberg](https://substack.com/redirect/d4a0670e-6158-4c53-bddd-9b7114227260?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)) all the more compelling. As long as I'm discussing Bushnell, I should note that the Israeli genocide in Gaza that compelled him to such dramatic dissent effectively [continues](https://substack.com/redirect/607446cb-0da0-4207-b7f1-14217ff81c05?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), despite the supposed ceasefire declared by the state and quasi-state actors involved.
Even within the U.S., the importance of Holsey's resignation as a Black admiral cannot be overstated.
The military was among the [first](https://substack.com/redirect/657f9056-e3cd-4bda-823d-bb2416f84831?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) public institutions in the U.S. to drive racial integration in a previous era. His departure renders the military less diverse, while also reinforcing a disturbing exodus of officers & servicemembers who come from marginalized racial & ethnic backgrounds or sexual orientations.
Observed through the lens of the present administration's attacks on Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion principles, Holsey's departure may have helped draw attention to a human rights and constitutional [crisis](https://substack.com/redirect/442e1741-f2d9-4312-a18b-d68bd6314a80?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--at the cost of the military's legacy as a racially integrated institution.

[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..5W0MGYWvK8H33Ao_2YiRlWFUyG1HsTkg2dpxmwKMgqY?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=181167648)
### **Why now?**
The outrage over these extrajudicial assassinations is about 15 years late.
It's also sadly convenient: the president who established the presidential power to kill without charge or trial continues to enjoy the adulation of liberals.
It was President Obama who [pioneered](https://substack.com/redirect/16ba92fe-44bd-40d5-b540-5d3fffc92da2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the practice, going so far as to personally approve what his administration claimed were "targeted" drone strikes.
The evidence, however, suggested otherwise. A joint [investigation](https://substack.com/redirect/66dca8d3-1382-415c-9b49-1f8659363715?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by international human rights clinics at Stanford and NYU law schools established in 2012 that drone strikes authorized by President Obama killed more unintended targets than intended ones. In other words, far from being in fact targeted, Obama's orders amounted to death sentences for people--particularly in Afghanistan--effectively chosen at random.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ef91399a-034a-49da-a887-cbd2cb4a3ffb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Source: [https://www.cnn.com/2016/04/01/politics/obama-isis-drone-strikes-iran](https://substack.com/redirect/ad97f327-95d1-43e5-8f2c-041ff8618590?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Particular outrage has seemed to emerge over the "double strike" pattern that killed a group of sailors in the Gulf of Mexico who reportedly survived an initial Pentagon strike. But, again, it was Obama who outrageously established that pattern.
What his administration called "double tap" strikes [frequently](https://substack.com/redirect/e20e73a5-b0a1-4973-91de-fb1b210d4326?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) aimed to kill first responders to people needing medical care in the wake of an initial bombing.
Those initial strikes were based on often faulty so-called "intelligence" with no process of any kind, let alone due process, to ensure accuracy in targeting decisions. They were precisely what Trump has been doing more recently, closer to home.
In 2011, I had a chance to deliver a [keynote address](https://substack.com/redirect/d3e2fe2f-c771-4816-9ff8-ac4c0b303d65?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) at a law symposium just down the street from where then-Attorney General Eric Holder had defended the Obama administration's use of arbitrary drone strikes just a few days earlier.
I am sad to observe that reason did not prevail. Many of us warned [at the time](https://substack.com/redirect/ead16ed0-b2c4-4f12-89e5-7a3d69675a81?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that Obama--who has more recently [criticized](https://substack.com/redirect/f9baf222-9268-45f3-b288-0278c182afd0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the rise of authoritarianism around the world--was creating a [roadmap](https://substack.com/redirect/d03855be-1f0c-4ee7-a398-68915c8bf2e6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for it here at home.
### Acronym soup is no defense
The only theoretical distinction one might offer between how Obama and Trump have used arbitrary drone strikes, aside from the location and hemisphere in which their choices have killed people chosen at random, is which agency they each deployed to commit their international crimes.
Obama reserved drone strikes for the infamously unaccountable [CIA](https://substack.com/redirect/5558c739-8427-49f7-abfc-f03e997f8158?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). As the world's most prolific terror organization, it makes sense for the CIA to be the agency committing international crimes. But it doesn't make it any more legal.
The CIA was also the agency principally responsible for [torture](https://substack.com/redirect/19668fd9-a597-4602-b13a-f8d3f2a4202c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), another bipartisan international crime for which no American decision makers have ever been held responsible. Beyond [insulating](https://substack.com/redirect/2c73627f-68f5-4745-b34f-fdbe27ce3bd9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Wall Street from accountability during the 2008 financial crisis and overseeing the violent suppression of the national grassroots Occupy movement that responded to it, Obama's most dangerous legacy was his decision to [keep secret](https://substack.com/redirect/31c794b2-8c03-4e18-b284-0278e70d19ec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the Senate investigation documenting the CIA's criminal trail, and to [look the other way](https://substack.com/redirect/f28bb755-ac18-48ed-a70c-ac28bb1cea57?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) rather than enforce the [legal precedent](https://substack.com/redirect/ba31791a-7161-462f-8c94-bb9f3aa07a08?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) established at Nuremberg.
Today, Trump & Hegseth deploy the Pentagon to commit the very same crimes.
They have [rebranded](https://substack.com/redirect/5207f79e-a3f3-4863-ac56-eed6c681e9d7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) it, changing its formal name from the Department of Defense into the Department of War. The overtly offensive insinuation of the Pentagon's new moniker (also its [old](https://substack.com/redirect/092f669a-d8fe-4f59-9669-144cc7894943?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) moniker) makes only more clear why any imagined distinction between the Pentagon and CIA is ephemeral.
From the standpoint of justice--embodied either in international law, or domestic constitutional principles--there is no distinction at all. Both agencies are American government institutions.
Their continuing crimes implicate everyone in Washington.
### **So what?**
Observing the bipartisan and interagency roots of this constitutional crime doesn't make it any better. It just indicates that we [can't vote](https://substack.com/redirect/d7bbbc7e-5cbb-4a38-ab0b-c5ff2f6e7ab9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) our way out of this problem. It also suggests which voices might have been paying attention more than the [partisan hacks](https://substack.com/redirect/b2ee3038-a80f-4a1d-93e1-72c18969a4d7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) who embrace authoritarianism whenever convenient to them and their party.
The bipartisan nature of these constitutional crimes also means that any impeachment targeting Hegseth would be under inclusive, because he is far from the only figure complicit in them.
As I said when I ran for Congress during Trump's first term (during a particularly crucial period of time [before](https://substack.com/redirect/eb684ff1-a0ec-41ca-be2f-e31c2ecd7aff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) senior Democrats were finally forced by grassroots pressure to embrace parts of my platform), impeachment [matters](https://substack.com/redirect/eb771390-911d-4880-a884-451c87ef5b6e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--but the grounds for impeachment are also important. Had Democrats in office followed the rest of my recommendations, Trump would have been impeached by the Senate and would never have returned to the White House this year.
But as I observed at the time, Democrats are [up to their necks](https://substack.com/redirect/f5ff4d15-659f-45cc-8e9d-b7fd0b671fd0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the same sordid self-serving offenses, which is why Americans shouldn't expect any better from them. It was Democrats, after all, who [established](https://substack.com/redirect/16ba92fe-44bd-40d5-b540-5d3fffc92da2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the dystopian presidential powers that Trump & Hegseth now abuse closer to home.
It was also [Democrats](https://substack.com/redirect/64929cd3-cd74-4d21-ab31-368752bb700d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) who [laughed](https://substack.com/redirect/7281711b-f79d-4e8e-af6c-4c44e696ed39?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) all the way to the bank for the past 40 years, filling their bulging pockets through insider trading practices that are illegal for anyone other than members of Congress.
### **They 're all in this together**
Trump [pardoning](https://substack.com/redirect/bdfa70b5-4b88-4764-9c8a-3286e21cf7ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) disgraced former Democratic Congressman Henry Cuellar offers a perfect example of how both parties are committed to corruption no less than the other. Corruption (in the 1.0 form of filling their filthy pockets) is, in fact, the one thing that most reliably brings them together (aside from weapon sales, which conveniently reflects [yet another dimension](https://substack.com/redirect/405d7087-c96f-4993-9f15-7562357aedce?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of corruption).
The political [kerfluffle](https://substack.com/redirect/14a0d9ef-2a53-492c-9232-8efbe2e1b61a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that has erupted between Trump and Cuellar in the time since Cuellar's pardon would be amusing were it not so revolting. The bipartisan poster children of corruption fighting amongst each other offers a perfect illustration of everything wrong with Washington.
_Paid subscribers can access a further discussion exploring how the geopolitical crisis in the Caribbean may be a distraction from another one brewing in the very same theater, a drama playing out in the relative shadows that might be driving the more visible one to which so many voices are responding..._

His resignation reflects a confronting military servicemembers, who face an international legal obligation to respect human rights, on the one hand, and conflicting courts-martial if they take action on that obligation by refusing the follow the chain of command. That conundrum should make the sacrifices of heroes like (or, for that matter, Edward Snowden or ) all the more compelling. As long as I'm discussing Bushnell, I should note that the Israeli genocide in Gaza that compelled him to such dramatic dissent effectively , despite the supposed ceasefire declared by the state and quasi-state actors involved. Even within the U.S., the importance of Holsey's resignation as a Black admiral cannot be overstated. The military was among the public institutions in the U.S. to drive racial integration in a previous era. His departure renders the military less diverse, while also reinforcing a disturbing exodus of officers & servicemembers who come from marginalized racial & ethnic backgrounds or sexual orientations. Observed through the lens of the present administration's attacks on Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion principles, Holsey's departure may have helped draw attention to a human rights and constitutional the cost of the military's legacy as a racially integrated institution. Chronicles of a Dying Empire is a publication made possible by readers. To receive new posts,sign up for a free subscription! Paid subscriptions help support my work, from which 21,000 readers learn for free. Why now? The outrage over these extrajudicial assassinations is about 15 years late. It's also sadly convenient: the president who established the presidential power to kill without charge or trial continues to enjoy the adulation of liberals. It was President Obama who the practice, going so far as to personally approve what his administration claimed were "targeted" drone strikes. The evidence, however, suggested otherwise. A joint by international human rights clinics at Stanford and NYU law schools established in 2012 that drone strikes authorized by President Obama killed more unintended targets than intended ones. In other words, far from being in fact targeted, Obama's orders amounted to death sentences for people--particularly in Afghanistan--effectively chosen at random. | | ---|---|--- Source: Particular outrage has seemed to emerge over the "double strike" pattern that killed a group of sailors in the Gulf of Mexico who reportedly survived an initial Pentagon strike. But, again, it was Obama who outrageously established that pattern. What his administration called "double tap" strikes aimed to kill first responders to people needing medical care in the wake of an initial bombing. Those initial strikes were based on often faulty so-called "intelligence" with no process of any kind, let alone due process, to ensure accuracy in targeting decisions. They were precisely what Trump has been doing more recently, closer to home. In 2011, I had a chance to deliver a at a law symposium just down the street from where then-Attorney General Eric Holder had defended the Obama administration's use of arbitrary drone strikes just a few days earlier. I am sad to observe that reason did not prevail. Many of us warned that Obama--who has more recently the rise of authoritarianism around the world--was creating a for it here at home. Acronym soup is no defense The only theoretical distinction one might offer between how Obama and Trump have used arbitrary drone strikes, aside from the location and hemisphere in which their choices have killed people chosen at random, is which agency they each deployed to commit their international crimes. Obama reserved drone strikes for the infamously unaccountable . As the world's most prolific terror organization, it makes sense for the CIA to be the agency committing international crimes. But it doesn't make it any more legal. The CIA was also the agency principally responsible for , another bipartisan international crime for which no American decision makers have ever been held responsible. Beyond Wall Street from accountability during the 2008 financial crisis and overseeing the violent suppression of the national grassroots Occupy movement that responded to it, Obama's most dangerous legacy was his decision to the Senate investigation documenting the CIA's criminal trail, and to rather than enforce the established at Nuremberg. Today, Trump & Hegseth deploy the Pentagon to commit the very same crimes. They have it, changing its formal name from the Department of Defense into the Department of War. The overtly offensive insinuation of the Pentagon's new moniker (also its moniker) makes only more clear why any imagined distinction between the Pentagon and CIA is ephemeral. From the standpoint of justice--embodied either in international law, or domestic constitutional principles--there is no distinction at all. Both agencies are American government institutions. Their continuing crimes implicate everyone in Washington. So what? Observing the bipartisan and interagency roots of this constitutional crime doesn't make it any better. It just indicates that we our way out of this problem. It also suggests which voices might have been paying attention more than the who embrace authoritarianism whenever convenient to them and their party. The bipartisan nature of these constitutional crimes also means that any impeachment targeting Hegseth would be under inclusive, because he is far from the only figure complicit in them. As I said when I ran for Congress during Trump's first term (during a particularly crucial period of time senior Democrats were finally forced by grassroots pressure to embrace parts of my platform), impeachment the grounds for impeachment are also important. Had Democrats in office followed the rest of my recommendations, Trump would have been impeached by the Senate and would never have returned to the White House this year. But as I observed at the time, Democrats are in the same sordid self-serving offenses, which is why Americans shouldn't expect any better from them. It was Democrats, after all, who the dystopian presidential powers that Trump & Hegseth now abuse closer to home. It was also who all the way to the bank for the past 40 years, filling their bulging pockets through insider trading practices that are illegal for anyone other than members of Congress. They 're all in this together Trump disgraced former Democratic Congressman Henry Cuellar offers a perfect example of how both parties are committed to corruption no less than the other. Corruption (in the 1.0 form of filling their filthy pockets) is, in fact, the one thing that most reliably brings them together (aside from weapon sales, which conveniently reflects of corruption). The political that has erupted between Trump and Cuellar in the time since Cuellar's pardon would be amusing were it not so revolting. The bipartisan poster children of corruption fighting amongst each other offers a perfect illustration of everything wrong with Washington. Paid subscribers can access a further discussion exploring how the geopolitical crisis in the Caribbean may be a distraction from another one brewing in the very same theater, a drama playing out in the relative shadows that might be driving the more visible one to which so many voices are responding... Fellow American,

I always think December is supposed to slow down. Family, faith, community - that's what this season is about for me.

But in campaigns? December is when everything **speeds up.**

**Here's the deal: the very last FEC End-of-Quarter report of the entire year closes in just a few weeks... and every reporter, every consultant, and every political opponent will be combing through those numbers to see whether _Team Cortez has the momentum to flip TX-35_.**

And honestly? I want to send a message.

Not a polite one.
A loud one.

A message that says: _**This movement is real, it's strong, and it's growing.**_

name: Team Ilhan
subject: Trump’s attacks have consequences
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ILHAN FOR CONGRESS
body:
These are the real-world results of reckless rhetoric
# [](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/35?t=1&akid=1990%2E791427%2E_mpsA3)
Friend, Trump’s constant attacks on Somali-Americans have consequences.
His lies, his conspiracies, and especially his threats of violent immigration crackdowns have sown fear and pain for no reason other than to scapegoat a vulnerable community.
A usually-bustling mall here in Minneapolis that offers hundreds of local Somali vendors was described as a “ghost city” this past weekend.
US citizens and long-term legal US residents alike are carrying passports and documents out of fear of being swept up in ICE’s ongoing racial profiling.
One Minnesota native of Somali descent told NBC News: **“Nobody is leaving their homes right now...** They don’t even have anything to run for. They’re citizens. They’ve been living here for years.”
Next door in Wisconsin, a Somali-American couple was harassed, attacked for their religion, and called horrible racial slurs while simply going out to eat.
And conservative “influencers” and far-right media have descended on Somali-American communities across the Midwest to spread hateful propaganda and harass regular people going about their lives.
**These are the real-world consequences of reckless rhetoric.** And we’ve seen it time and time again from Trump. He’s attacked Mexicans, El Salvadorans, Haitians, Venezuelans, Yemenis, Palestinians, Afghans, all Muslims, and now Somali-Americans.
As Ilhan has said, Trump has to lash out like this to distract us from his failures. It’s his entire playbook: He pits working-class people of different backgrounds against each other to keep us from uniting against him and the billionaire class.
But thankfully, **the American people know better.** Despite the extremists on the fringe, we’ve seen an outpouring of support from across the country. Americans understand that immigrants built this country, made it what it is, and continue to contribute to our economy and our social fabric.
So thank you to everyone who stands in solidarity with Ilhan Omar, Minneapolis, and the Somali-American community.
We won’t stop fighting for the better world we know is possible. We won’t stop calling out racism and standing by our neighbors. We will defeat hate and division and put forward a positive agenda that lifts everybody up.
— Team Ilhan HQ
* * *

**Before you go,** consider chipping in to Ilhan Omar’s campaign today. We are coming up on a big FEC fundraising deadline, and we want to prove that a huge grassroots movement is standing by Ilhan as she takes on Trump. Give $10 or whatever you can afford here:
**[CHIP IN $10](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-dec25.HnwiDc&refcode=em251209-1-180&t=2&akid=1990%2E791427%2E_mpsA3)**

PAID FOR BY ILHAN FOR CONGRESS
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a very important way we communicate with supporters like you, but if you want to, you can **[unsubscribe here](https://act.ilhanomar.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?source=em-footer&t=3&akid=1990%2E791427%2E_mpsA3)**. You can also **[sign up to receive fewer emails](https://act.ilhanomar.com/signup/fewer-emails/?source=em-footer&t=4&akid=1990%2E791427%2E_mpsA3)**.
Submit this form to [**update your contact information**](https://act.ilhanomar.com/signup/update-contact-info/source=em-footer?t=5&akid=1990%2E791427%2E_mpsA3)
Ilhan is counting on our people-powered grassroots movement to fund this campaign. If you can, stand with us by **[making a contribution today](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/io-em-footer/?refcode=em-footer&t=6&refcode2=1990_791427__mpsA3&akid=1990%2E791427%2E_mpsA3)**.
Ilhan for Congress
PO Box 33079
Washington D.C., 20033

name: Lauren Toncich, Turning Point USA
subject: I can still hear Charlie backstage...
body:
The one event Charlie loved most...| | |
---
| | Hi Peter,

My name is Lauren Toncich -- and for the past seven years, I've helped lead the team behind the curtain at every Turning Point USA event.

Making sure the lights hit at the right moment, the music is pumping through the crowd, and tens of thousands of people walk into something unforgettable.

I've never emailed you directly before.

But if you've ever seen the opening night of AmericaFest... felt the electricity of a Student Action Summit crowd... or watched one of our massive rallies ignite a room...

Then you've witnessed the kind of energy Charlie dreamed of -- brought to life by our incredible events team.
---
[](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=3718d1b65a414b8f99572f5f31dc09b2&_e=QmlW1RZYtt7llTobnup4DlkbI71MFrDS3p-K9h2IP_HQiW0tL76nb7OfVRXIh0oz0BqA5qbcrVmlhXMV5qhnEoiH0JrAUjItMWPN5W9L3DdDRu1hviEx6UTMDiSvZ-OW0FbTBWU-Z4oQTx9uqTfJ60E2087EJCzEbX287rbapODZYdOY8NyHmkshrojnKbBLVgArZUwj3BGKSVosi9H04Y3np8AYFnNNi63-81KI-5AlmE1O2Po2nhAVyf0I9I8QT9RSrh_2aHAgSsOPrmNreX04QBwYfQepGiKVRj7BHlOGI2d5Ajycx6s4sF_xmQj2lSd3xAfaADngUfOhuKfoHJ0gnlgzypPrSyz0L50Q4vzoJO1ZQm2K7tqyXRKCyHSIR7P0BnB8wPB5WnaoWQBQZZs-Nf3YnfPd01ldEvTUEVay46Uj8lseuzKcOdixKzVegLQvenWPejhxVqF7qHex_V6scEKkPAC-nbQQiJa1zfDU5FQOw1BtEhh3lFxCizdB9-H0yFM8TmsUm3MAutw1Moan5-3qVW2lURXfzi2CXwxShgilo1iocx0dizCK0ySnphJLm52aLoBDD8BpErx8xUAhC-bg5BO6lGmCv9aW50TVzqx34rc612xrjWsFT2Dw-EWgRcxkoobNRlFQUeudW06m1FzuZreQRWAn9ZlACSjhVKRSH4gqOI2pi2xPi3R9)
---
_Above: That 's me with Charlie, backstage at last year's AmFest making sure every detail was perfect before showtime. _
---
At the heart of every single Turning Point event is this one truth: _Young Americans deserve a stage worthy of the fight they 're in. _

**And AmericaFest -- especially this year -- is our most important stage of all. **

**This December 18 -21 in Phoenix, thousands of students will walk into the most powerful conservative gathering in America. **

It will be our first AmFest without Charlie.

This is the stage he built -- with vision, grit, and sky-high standards.

Charlie poured everything into these events, because he believed students deserved more than beige ballrooms and lazy lectures.

He believed they needed to _FEEL_ the mission -- That they were part of something unstoppable... something big enough to change the world.

Backstage, I can still hear him directing the show: _" Louder music... more sparks... bigger energy... how full are we looking?" _

Not because he wanted flash for the sake of flash...

But because he knew energy moves people. Environments shape conviction.

_And atmosphere matters when you 're calling students into the fight of their lives. _
---
[](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=3718d1b65a414b8f99572f5f31dc09b2&_e=QmlW1RZYtt7llTobnup4DlkbI71MFrDS3p-K9h2IP_HQiW0tL76nb7OfVRXIh0oz0BqA5qbcrVmlhXMV5qhnEoiH0JrAUjItMWPN5W9L3DdDRu1hviEx6UTMDiSvZ-OW0FbTBWU-Z4oQTx9uqTfJ60E2087EJCzEbX287rbapODZYdOY8NyHmkshrojnKbBLVgArZUwj3BGKSVosi9H04Y3np8AYFnNNi63-81KI-5Aj19SBiplpG3NLIA-wHXxytabM9GHfiY-m3whqjoghOFg8ks4R3cqueS0u7hfrB2pb3WN8j7_XrPROan0H3sasznZ2CqRD0RA4zuOGR36ph8j1mWeTXGjOglwP7OJ9lWqR2jyWjDS-zGyfclAcu2u5Gf87LB0kzx-PeT7877cvmHAgwRfIQIXadNm1mo-cFttX8zOLje1oJU1diZAz6v4R-mGz6MRJiuokeVvYYjkFgHR4iOcelo9NRGEfnpoggLpsgYgkhidcyTRXKx3sbyoEPF2zHqMMdHG9DN7wEnD-aXUNviXKXCvr3PSrlidxBPaMI-irmiFY6ihiz4f6gMgJhG9W7ZjIw6O5i_DT0XSKBv29WBiTVBYGw69hnoN38KWo_xSUvXEqbWGva1lrHRth1ZGEpAdw_awMm2A3fb2pEP7BErv_vfRW03KBmroRRLnlVtDaD0BNvpbGHGspQm_W)
---
This year's AmFest?

_I 've never seen more students eager to be part of it. _

**Nearly 6,000 are registered. Another 2,200 are on the waitlist. **

**And we only have a few days left to get them in. **

Peter, these students aren't looking for entertainment...

They're looking for courage, purpose, and a movement to belong to.

They're stepping onto hostile campuses. They're getting flak from Leftist professors.

Many are leading Turning Point chapters in places where they're told to sit down, shut up -- or worse.

**They want to stand for America... _They just need a chance to stand with others who will, too. _**

And when they get to AmFest, it's not just the main stage that changes their lives.

Behind the lights and cameras, they sit in smaller breakout rooms -- face-to-face with staff and speakers who show them exactly how to lead.
---
[](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=3718d1b65a414b8f99572f5f31dc09b2&_e=QmlW1RZYtt7llTobnup4DlkbI71MFrDS3p-K9h2IP_HQiW0tL76nb7OfVRXIh0oz0BqA5qbcrVmlhXMV5qhnEoiH0JrAUjItMWPN5W9L3DdDRu1hviEx6UTMDiSvZ-OW0FbTBWU-Z4oQTx9uqTfJ60E2087EJCzEbX287rbapODZYdOY8NyHmkshrojnKbBLVgArZUwj3BGKSVosi9H04Y3np8AYFnNNi63-81KI-5Dl4uehB80r-bKcjdPo4TZE0cMlAzMwvxbupVyItbT-wEriFWmuPRi-9HqbY6J0Z-iCP3qYfBJxsw7JRaLp4rDnwf2Bt0iVUvjpeUl1kxlQa47CUQR9V_xN52L03yAtygl8THbB10oJfw9ZyYXtuptGlmVYQ1gGb6Euk8u-PFSSft2_Bf4bZ5zItg_IOm3BX0Zf-t6YPW7T1vxLGRvqNHP5MPOz29Y1-CqqHeKp8ldyVX0_sn5iEf5Y4bbd9bZWk13IXvLG2Ml9gAkoJ-yi-d_QZAr_op6_kwRbwYzJaz9BI3zMaQIvrpQEci-pWh9jB12Pppu4iW-AWKULr6mBt9c7M_x8551V3QtjOSSarT4FHR672L69Cz_xV6FX1kuVHMhgHgLcDe2-aivHYE7sy6ZDuG2djFBftrDtpm5SXfIShEtFfi4a3DJjCWxZ7o3g6Y_V326gMgoC31Nt4E8X2s03)
---
They learn how to organize. How to launch and grow chapters. How to push back against the Left -- strategically and fearlessly.

They're trained to fight -- and win.

Because inspiration is just the beginning. We want every student walking out of AmFest equipped for the battlefield they're stepping into.

**Peter, that 's where you can make a difference. **

**Every $50 you give today helps sponsor a student to attend AmericaFest. And today, your gift will be MATCHED -- dollar-for-dollar. **

That means:

🔥 $50 sends TWO students
🔥 $100 sends FOUR
🔥 $250 sends EIGHT, and more.

clean_body: The one event Charlie loved most...| | | | | Hi Peter, My name is Lauren Toncich -- and for the past seven years, I've helped lead the team behind the curtain at every Turning Point USA event. Making sure the lights hit at the right moment, the music is pumping through the crowd, and tens of thousands of people walk into something unforgettable. I've never emailed you directly before. But if you've ever seen the opening night of AmericaFest... felt the electricity of a Student Action Summit crowd... or watched one of our massive rallies ignite a room... Then you've witnessed the kind of energy Charlie dreamed of -- brought to life by our incredible events team. Above: That 's me with Charlie, backstage at last year's AmFest making sure every detail was perfect before showtime. At the heart of every single Turning Point event is this one truth: Young Americans deserve a stage worthy of the fight they 're in. And AmericaFest -- especially this year -- is our most important stage of all. This December 18 -21 in Phoenix, thousands of students will walk into the most powerful conservative gathering in America. It will be our first AmFest without Charlie. This is the stage he built -- with vision, grit, and sky-high standards. Charlie poured everything into these events, because he believed students deserved more than beige ballrooms and lazy lectures. He believed they needed to FEEL the mission -- That they were part of something unstoppable... something big enough to change the world. Backstage, I can still hear him directing the show: " Louder music... more sparks... bigger energy... how full are we looking?" Not because he wanted flash for the sake of flash... But because he knew energy moves people. Environments shape conviction. And atmosphere matters when you 're calling students into the fight of their lives. This year's AmFest? I 've never seen more students eager to be part of it. Nearly 6,000 are registered. Another 2,200 are on the waitlist. And we only have a few days left to get them in. Peter, these students aren't looking for entertainment... They're looking for courage, purpose, and a movement to belong to. They're stepping onto hostile campuses. They're getting flak from Leftist professors. Many are leading Turning Point chapters in places where they're told to sit down, shut up -- or worse. They want to stand for America... They just need a chance to stand with others who will, too. And when they get to AmFest, it's not just the main stage that changes their lives. Behind the lights and cameras, they sit in smaller breakout rooms -- face-to-face with staff and speakers who show them exactly how to lead. They learn how to organize. How to launch and grow chapters. How to push back against the Left -- strategically and fearlessly. They're trained to fight -- and win. Because inspiration is just the beginning. We want every student walking out of AmFest equipped for the battlefield they're stepping into. Peter, that 's where you can make a difference. Every $50 you give today helps sponsor a student to attend AmericaFest. And today, your gift will be MATCHED -- dollar-for-dollar. That means: 🔥 $50 sends TWO students 🔥 $100 sends FOUR 🔥 $250 sends EIGHT, and more. Every year, I see it firsthand: Students walk in with doubts, with fears, with barely enough money to afford the flight. They walk out with confidence, connection, and a new sense of calling. I've seen them go home and start chapters. I've seen them run for office. Many tell me -- even years later, that this one event changed everything. So no -- I'm not just asking you to sponsor an "event." I 'm asking you to help change the trajectory of a young patriot. Peter, I know what Charlie would want right now. He'd want every seat filled. He'd want AmFest to echo with boldness and fire. He'd want us to fight like he's still backstage -- still calling for more sparks. Most of all, he'd want every student who made the trip to believe they were part of something bigger than themselves. To walk in searching for purpose -- and leave filled with it. . Let's make sure every young patriot who wants to fight for this country gets their chance -- and gets to Phoenix this December. | | For Charlie, Lauren Toncich Vice President of Events Turning Point USA P.S. This is our first AmericaFest without Charlie. But it will be the most important one we've ever hosted. Help us fill the room he loved -- with students ready to carry his mission forward. 4940 East Beverly Road Phoenix, Arizona 85044

I planned to wait until tomorrow to publish, but something I saw last night changed that. *There will still be a post tomorrow morning @ 06:09*
Last night I saw something that hit me in a way I did not expect. It felt insulting. It felt petty. It felt deliberate.
The DCCC posted a graphic using the exact slogan I ran on for two years.
**Make Congress Work Again.**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/eba1f32c-04b7-4db7-b086-6746978865b7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Not something similar.
Not something adjacent.
**The same words. The same message. The same font style and layout I used through 2024.**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/73581dc4-c951-42b8-a8a7-d3a70f77d10b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
They never asked.
They never emailed.
They never engaged with my campaign when it mattered.
But suddenly, out of nowhere, they blast it out on their national account.
Instead of supporting candidates everywhere, is this what the DCCC is doing to other Dems they ignore?
* * *
# **When It Mattered**
Let's be clear about what happened here.
The DCCC did not support in 2022.
The DCCC did not support in 2024.
They did not answer emails, calls, or outreach. They were silent.
Meanwhile, I was running in one of the few competitive districts left in Ohio.
Running against Max Miller, a hard-right Trump loyalist.
A man who went on television advocating for turning Gaza into a parking lot.
A guy who had public scandals and legal messes unfolding in real time.
A seat the party should have cared about.
And then Dennis Kucinich jumped in as an independent spoiler.
You would think at that point the national party would wake up and realize the race was on fire.
Nope.
No help.
No interest.
No acknowledgment.
But they somehow had no problem using the slogan I built, the slogan on my hats, my signs, my banners, my emails, my stump speeches, my website, my social media, everything my campaign stood for.
Now it shows up on their page like it was theirs the whole time.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/0fe3f6b4-a762-4811-bbbf-1498971bf7dc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* * *
# **It Feels Like a Message**
I am not claiming I am a national political figure.
I am not claiming I have massive reach.
But the specificity of this is too sharp to ignore.
The feeling this creates is simple:
**We saw your work.
We ignored you when you were the nominee.
We waited.
And then we used the slogan anyway.**
It feels intentional.
It feels dismissive.
It feels like the party establishment saying,
**" We were watching. We just didn't care to help."**
Is this provable as an act of malice? No.
But the emotional truth is valid, and the facts support that it is extremely unlikely they arrived at the exact slogan with exact font and color by complete accident.
* * *
# **They Have Borrowed From My Messaging Before**
This is not the first time I have watched the Democratic establishment ignore my campaign and then quietly recycle the very messaging they once dismissed.
Back in 2022, consultants told me not to talk about China, not to mention the national debt, not to bring up fiscal responsibility. They said those ideas were off-brand, that they "didn't poll well," that a Democrat shouldn't touch those topics. Then Tim Ryan ran for Senate and built half his campaign around that exact message.
In 2024, I ran on working-class economics, kitchen-table issues, affordability, government accountability, and institutional reform. I talked about the cost of living, healthcare failures, national debt, and the squeeze on ordinary families. Those were the issues people in Northeast Ohio actually cared about, and I said so every day on the trail.
The national party ignored it.
They told candidates to stay in familiar lanes.
They acted like talking about real economic pain was somehow risky and anti-Biden.
And then, after losing ground with working-class voters across the Midwest and the Sun Belt, they suddenly pivoted to the exact same issues I had been pounding away at for two cycles.
This is a pattern.
They ignore you when it matters.
They adopt the message only after they realize they needed it.
And somehow, they still pretend the ideas came from inside the building.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/88e877bd-ca8c-47f1-974b-f7706683d879?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* * *
# **This Is About Integrity**
Let me be crystal clear.
I am not mad because they used the words. (TBH I learned it was used in the 2010's. And since 2023, my campaign, it has been used a lot of other places.) I am mad because they ignored the work, the race, the district, and the people.
If the Democratic Party wants to win, then start by respecting the people who actually show up and fight the hard fights. Do not wait until after the race is over to pretend you were part of it.
And stop punishing candidates because they think independently.
I do not care if a good idea comes from the left, the right, or the middle.
If it helps working families, we should talk about it.
We should fight for it.
We should pass it.
I ran on making Congress work again because Congress does not work.
Not for Democrats.
Not for Republicans.
Not for anyone who is not a donor, a lobbyist, or a corporate PAC.
Our slogan was about giving people a voice.
Whether or not some intern at the DCCC ripped off my exact slogan doesn't bother me that much. What does bother me is the culture of bad ideas, shady consultants, dark money and crony politics that has brought the Democratic party to it's knees.
**Make DCCC Work Again!!**
_Join other**ANGRY DEMOCRATS** by support Matt's work. Subscribe and Share! _

And then Dennis Kucinich jumped in as an independent spoiler. You would think at that point the national party would wake up and realize the race was on fire. Nope. No help. No interest. No acknowledgment. But they somehow had no problem using the slogan I built, the slogan on my hats, my signs, my banners, my emails, my stump speeches, my website, my social media, everything my campaign stood for. Now it shows up on their page like it was theirs the whole time. | | ---|---|--- It Feels Like a Message I am not claiming I am a national political figure. I am not claiming I have massive reach. But the specificity of this is too sharp to ignore. The feeling this creates is simple: We saw your work. We ignored you when you were the nominee. We waited. And then we used the slogan anyway. It feels intentional. It feels dismissive. It feels like the party establishment saying, " We were watching. We just didn't care to help." Is this provable as an act of malice? No. But the emotional truth is valid, and the facts support that it is extremely unlikely they arrived at the exact slogan with exact font and color by complete accident. They Have Borrowed From My Messaging Before This is not the first time I have watched the Democratic establishment ignore my campaign and then quietly recycle the very messaging they once dismissed. Back in 2022, consultants told me not to talk about China, not to mention the national debt, not to bring up fiscal responsibility. They said those ideas were off-brand, that they "didn't poll well," that a Democrat shouldn't touch those topics. Then Tim Ryan ran for Senate and built half his campaign around that exact message. In 2024, I ran on working-class economics, kitchen-table issues, affordability, government accountability, and institutional reform. I talked about the cost of living, healthcare failures, national debt, and the squeeze on ordinary families. Those were the issues people in Northeast Ohio actually cared about, and I said so every day on the trail. The national party ignored it. They told candidates to stay in familiar lanes. They acted like talking about real economic pain was somehow risky and anti-Biden. And then, after losing ground with working-class voters across the Midwest and the Sun Belt, they suddenly pivoted to the exact same issues I had been pounding away at for two cycles. This is a pattern. They ignore you when it matters. They adopt the message only after they realize they needed it. And somehow, they still pretend the ideas came from inside the building. | | ---|---|--- This Is About Integrity Let me be crystal clear. I am not mad because they used the words. (TBH I learned it was used in the 2010's. And since 2023, my campaign, it has been used a lot of other places.) I am mad because they ignored the work, the race, the district, and the people. If the Democratic Party wants to win, then start by respecting the people who actually show up and fight the hard fights. Do not wait until after the race is over to pretend you were part of it. And stop punishing candidates because they think independently. I do not care if a good idea comes from the left, the right, or the middle. If it helps working families, we should talk about it. We should fight for it. We should pass it. I ran on making Congress work again because Congress does not work. Not for Democrats. Not for Republicans. Not for anyone who is not a donor, a lobbyist, or a corporate PAC. Our slogan was about giving people a voice. Whether or not some intern at the DCCC ripped off my exact slogan doesn't bother me that much. What does bother me is the culture of bad ideas, shady consultants, dark money and crony politics that has brought the Democratic party to it's knees. Make DCCC Work Again!! Join otherANGRY DEMOCRATS** by support Matt's work. Subscribe and Share! | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Matt Diemer 548 Market Street PMB 72296, San Francisco, CA 94104 ? ?

subject: The real con is you, Trump

body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251209_affordability_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251209_affordability_act]
I saw Trump’s comments last week about affordability being a Democratic “con job.” But you and I both know: the real con job is the President’s refusal to act while everyday families are struggling to survive.
When he ran for President, he promised to bring prices down on day one, and that didn’t happen.
Sky-high prices are a crisis. Families in St. Louis are being forced to choose between rent, groceries, healthcare, and keeping the lights on.
Being able to get our basic needs taken care of is what is at stake, and Trump and his allies don’t care. They continue to ignore the reality of people, even as costs rise and billionaires become even richer.
I’m running for Congress to fight for our district, tackle affordability head-on, and ensure that families aren’t forced to make impossible choices. I’ve been fighting for people my whole career, and I’m not backing down now.
Listen, I can’t win without you. Trump and his megadonor allies, the same ones calling affordability a con, are pouring money into protecting the status quo and enriching themselves while our families suffer.
We refuse to let that happen and your support powers our movement. Once elected, I’ll do the job that Trump and his allies refuse to do. They’ve got money, but we’ve got time and energy. If we invest right now, we can build a campaign to take them on, but it starts today. Can you chip in $20 or whatever you can afford today? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251209_affordability_act&amount=20]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
DONATE $5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=x_251209_affordability_act] DONATE $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=x_251209_affordability_act]
DONATE $25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=x_251209_affordability_act] DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=x_251209_affordability_act]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?express_lane=true&amount=100&refcode=x_251209_affordability_act] OTHER [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?express_lane_other=true&refcode=x_251209_affordability_act]
In solidarity,
— Cori
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251209_affordability_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251209_affordability_act] Cori Bush is a pastor, nurse, survivor, and formerly unhoused single mother of two who knows what it means to struggle and what it means to rise .
As a Congresswoman, she has always shown up when it’s hardest: securing disaster relief, lowering insulin costs, and delivering millions for our communities.
Across St. Louis, families are being priced out and pushed out. Cori Bush is fighting back for affordable housing, fair wages, and utilities as a human right. Be part of this people-first movement. Visit CoriBush.org [https://www.coribush.org] to learn more.
DONATE TODAY [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251209_affordability_act]

St. Louis, MO 63132
United States

name: Mandela Barnes
subject: Scott Walker tried to call me out
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mandela for Wisconsin
body:
But he didn’t try to deny our strength.
| Well friend, this one’s a doozy. Scott Walker just called me “radical” on social media. But in the same post, he wrote **“That said, he is the front-runner in his primary, and he could win the general election.”** I don’t think expanding Badgercare or supporting our farmers is radical, but Scott and I are on the same page about one thing: with supporters like you in my corner, we’re going to win this race. **[Will you help me keep up the momentum with a contribution of any amount that is meaningful to you?](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/1212?c0=mb-em-fr-dec25.SSbw4W&amount=15&refcode=SBD_MB_EM_FR_120925_Walker_Tier1_D1_S1&t=2&akid=5701%2E196590%2Egqzoop)** _If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process now._ | | | [DONATE $15 »](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/6574?amount=15&refcode=SBD_MB_EM_FR_120925_Walker_Tier1_D1_S1&t=3&akid=5701%2E196590%2Egqzoop)

---
| [OTHER AMOUNT »](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/6598?c0=SBD_MB_EM_FR_120925_Walker_Tier1_D1_S1.BBpz9N&t=8&akid=5701%2E196590%2Egqzoop)
---
Just last week, my Republican opponent called me a “dangerous far-left extremist.” Now, the former governor called me a “radical.”
But I’m not backing down. Making it easier for families to make ends meet is just common sense. That’s why we’re leading in this race, and even Republicans can’t deny the strength of our movement.
**[Can I count on you for $15 or $27 to keep up our momentum in the second week of this campaign?](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/1212?c0=mb-em-fr-dec25.SSbw4W&amount=15&refcode=SBD_MB_EM_FR_120925_Walker_Tier1_D1_S1&t=9&akid=5701%2E196590%2Egqzoop)**
Thank you for standing with me,
Mandela Barnes

| | [ CONTRIBUTE NOW » ](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/6525?source_code=&t=1&akid=5701%2E196590%2Egqzoop)

clean_body: We can’t believe we’re almost at the end of Emily’s first year in Congress! | Peter, we can’t believe we’re almost at the end of Emily’s first year in Congress! We put together a highlight reel from the past twelve months – please take a minute to watch and share: Some of our favorite moments include: ➡️ Connecting with thousands of neighbors at 16 town halls, both in-person and online. ➡️ Traveling to meetings across the 6th District by bus, bike, ferry, and even hiking trails. ➡️ Introducing House colleagues to Bad Bunny! 🐶 (He’s a big morale booster.) Make no mistake: This was a tough year for our neighbors and our country. Our team will be working hard over the next 11 months to hold the Trump regime and House Republicans accountable while also electing a new generation of leaders who will deliver for working people. But today, we hope you enjoy this compilation from this rollercoaster of a year – please like, comment, and share on your platform of choice! Thank you for being a part of Team Emily 💙 – the whole team at Emily Randall for Congress = | | Emily Randall is proud to represent Washington’s 6th District in Congress. She grew up in a working-class Union household in Port Orchard and was the first in her family to attend college. After serving in the State Senate and passing nearly 200 bills, Emily made history in 2024 as the first queer Latina elected to Congress. Now, Emily is fighting for WA working families in D.C. and organizing to take back the House. Can you chip in $6 or any amount and join our team? = Want to maximize your impact?Become a monthly donor to help us flip the House from red to blue! = Follow on: | | | | | | Paid for by Emily Randall for Congress Emily Randall for Congress P.O. Box 1883 Port Orchard, WA 98366 United States | | If you would like to receive only our most important messages, . body:
Reclaiming our future
The values that guide me are simple: protect families, defend faith, and safeguard freedom.

| | Hi Fellow Patriot,
---
My name is **Victor Marx** -- a husband of 37 years, father of five, grandfather, Marine Corps veteran, former pastor, and humanitarian who has spent decades serving families and communities around the world. **_I am also the conservative in the race for Governor of Colorado._** I'm running because the challenges we're facing here reflect the challenges facing our entire nation. What happens in one state can strengthen -- _or weaken_ -- the future of America. [**That’s why I’m reaching out today and asking you to become a key early part of our rapidly growing grassroots movement.**](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=25579b7059c0460886ba3ef282e5190d&_e=-TsvspNW6_D40yJLtWWk9Pv0MV90HVEBAdXvL6qTU_H_e4ouaJxTaB0jB3l2x4n9qpDpdR2D9vXPCN5yEUOtH4eO_QQ3TJRqomCAMwV3_4mHGPRgdkhiN3L3KhnePzLrtsWQGxTWXlBR8fcBEbgDbWgSVA9lsGGuUDtCxIWZYEmvJnusxQ-zqRl8oASzDaoL7s5sEctGST7mk-RhGZR8rxVYQZg8Nw4nSwT0BtZVxGmDt5cusac9P9IIc8Pw6sj9Zc3kj9qs2JC8ULvg_AsZonqMVdZmIOVqedtG_8RHAvua4kjFf8diYfkHfIXsz9nPu-_MB5atCa_yzglJwHxCzaxyqgbtyI7-TiPayWfD6tBTH2CQNT0_dfQNpPgeEOvbgqIH6_r3MkmyRZC88Vc8DuEUl_FFotL2YICGeL53Ky3yTYsvFpRbEaV7uvePmPhOJDO0110SihpoW2lH0Uqpm7VENacnCUsTFCTIM1YyOClV8p0eEcvR1b2l8llYtoXu2ucCL_64hbkTyPl4CTwQg1oE5JTcpW6Q7PJWfkXudxFgFmIZw0EcM-Zx2kuVRdzZjWtEg1MK37IZCFTn0L18apIXsg_4R9uRYt6HxlEFsgLeJtJZDiqh92jv01zPekHS)

For years, my calling has taken me into some of the most difficult places imaginable. From war zones to trafficking corridors to communities devastated by violence, **I 've led more than 150 high-risk missions focused on rescuing and restoring the innocent.** That work exists because **God carried me** through a childhood marked by trauma, instability, and hardship. Through His grace, I rebuilt my life -- and I've spent every day since fighting for those who can't fight for themselves. **_This campaign is no different._** It isn't powered by insiders or special interests. It's powered by people who still believe in Faith, Family, Freedom, and the promise of a stronger America. We're already building real momentum, and we need to keep it going. I want you with us in this fight, [**and a contribution from you of $25, $50 or even $100 will help us continue building a movement that can win here and keep spreading.**](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=25579b7059c0460886ba3ef282e5190d&_e=-TsvspNW6_D40yJLtWWk9Pv0MV90HVEBAdXvL6qTU_H_e4ouaJxTaB0jB3l2x4n9qpDpdR2D9vXPCN5yEUOtH4eO_QQ3TJRqomCAMwV3_4mHGPRgdkhiN3L3KhnePzLrtsWQGxTWXlBR8fcBEbgDbWgSVA9lsGGuUDtCxIWZYEmvJnusxQ-zqRl8oASzDaoL7s5sEctGST7mk-RhGZR8r_GBmYRCKvahdnltA_XkZj1xL5XSHFav2_dLsOpaKjWk3mfeaVrvP3sCoCpFk2mBLOzbGdfSdwi2IbSqMGsQ9fA8Y5nyIiWdHe9G77gFq0azjgcbxVMPAG63hVyRyHN7YRso3oEyIlBmGtNqf0HGc1MAekkPTdzJJ113_p3XDvkncX-elesNvJ--vcTZAi5I-sO_Cgf9ecqJaAM-mSp2RWd4AFfG4iVtF4hAt58660MBGMg9-Mbg1VJsQSrvk3zzlwB_-_XR6vj7B4FYyf6tu3_3ho9h1ISDU5bNs1Cgps5zDyzY_FIQKkxng5XMvHZQpcYwk262rx5e0zUJJPIW52kkQwpF1aCumucPAkEqBCw1Yp6I4PbvL1y37W4pCpsIrrvxKieiLPFqrNUIO36c9f7gf_b6ajA7sn2XyL-L4MXS)

Over the years, I've been blessed to walk alongside men and women of deep conviction--people who don't just speak about change but embody it in the way they live. One of those rare leaders I held in the highest regard was my friend, the late **Charlie Kirk.** After Charlie passed, his wife Erika told me that Charlie would want me to run, and she encouraged me to throw my hat in the ring. While I've never considered myself a politician--_nor do I intend to become one_ --I reached a point where I couldn't ignore the reality that our communities are being failed. **I knew I had to do something. ** _Thankfully_ , I'm not stepping into this fight alone. Thousands of patriots have already taken their place in this movement, [**and I’m counting on you to stand with us.**](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=25579b7059c0460886ba3ef282e5190d&_e=-TsvspNW6_D40yJLtWWk9Pv0MV90HVEBAdXvL6qTU_H_e4ouaJxTaB0jB3l2x4n9qpDpdR2D9vXPCN5yEUOtH4eO_QQ3TJRqomCAMwV3_4mHGPRgdkhiN3L3KhnePzLrtsWQGxTWXlBR8fcBEbgDbWgSVA9lsGGuUDtCxIWZYEmvJnusxQ-zqRl8oASzDaoL7s5sEctGST7mk-RhGZR8r6HgCucG89MP2GODaF9aDOBGMm4a4tzVZFPjJo4ERYLz3Aeiu4QTJ7RmIxfDnmgikaSqUVW8NpBu9ZmXuoOb_WvNnSB0W3TQJbsceeWyBchbI4vi4GSPZ4MmfPIYHOcpIahe5mhHjO2d0aXDsVcJSThWMdmqLkHb6pfm7RhUIOY6KDvgNMcsi9Yrc679XaK83zm40pzDM95n0B8TPHoKBSYJRRd1BO9dV2zxoDBz21SvUnJ8CpkWUuXjWrUnaB2f4DqA7hRBPmff0WLHwSTaZ4tEgE2_hYQRDbJ9vITisZdkU0CgW8OEefgSUIoPnTjlb4OyJCD7x4K-uLHIZKDryLrLzmqxCSL3Mi1BxxskGPU378f_a9aa3WK1ECKCMsxCjjEv__oJiTtDfzv-kDUOrE3iFyFQl0av_Fcys2eDfP0e)

The values that guide me are simple: **protect families, defend faith, safeguard freedom, and steward the natural beauty we 've been entrusted with. **These are not just Colorado values -- _they 're American values._ The struggles we face here -- crime, government overreach, attacks on faith, rising costs -- are the same challenges pressing on families across the country. Turning Colorado around sends a message far beyond our borders: **that courage still matters, that conviction still wins, and that ordinary Americans can reclaim their future.** I believe deeply in that mission. _I 've lived it my entire life._ And I'm grateful to have so many determined patriots standing alongside me as we work toward a safer, stronger, more united future. clean_body: Reclaiming our future The values that guide me are simple: protect families, defend faith, and safeguard freedom. | | | | | Hi Fellow Patriot, My name is Victor Marx -- a husband of 37 years, father of five, grandfather, Marine Corps veteran, former pastor, and humanitarian who has spent decades serving families and communities around the world. I am also the conservative in the race for Governor of Colorado. I'm running because the challenges we're facing here reflect the challenges facing our entire nation. What happens in one state can strengthen -- or weaken -- the future of America. For years, my calling has taken me into some of the most difficult places imaginable. From war zones to trafficking corridors to communities devastated by violence, I 've led more than 150 high-risk missions focused on rescuing and restoring the innocent. That work exists because God carried me through a childhood marked by trauma, instability, and hardship. Through His grace, I rebuilt my life -- and I've spent every day since fighting for those who can't fight for themselves. This campaign is no different. It isn't powered by insiders or special interests. It's powered by people who still believe in Faith, Family, Freedom, and the promise of a stronger America. We're already building real momentum, and we need to keep it going. I want you with us in this fight, Over the years, I've been blessed to walk alongside men and women of deep conviction--people who don't just speak about change but embody it in the way they live. One of those rare leaders I held in the highest regard was my friend, the late Charlie Kirk. After Charlie passed, his wife Erika told me that Charlie would want me to run, and she encouraged me to throw my hat in the ring. While I've never considered myself a politician--nor do I intend to become one --I reached a point where I couldn't ignore the reality that our communities are being failed. I knew I had to do something. Thankfully , I'm not stepping into this fight alone. Thousands of patriots have already taken their place in this movement, The values that guide me are simple: protect families, defend faith, safeguard freedom, and steward the natural beauty we 've been entrusted with. These are not just Colorado values -- they 're American values. The struggles we face here -- crime, government overreach, attacks on faith, rising costs -- are the same challenges pressing on families across the country. Turning Colorado around sends a message far beyond our borders: that courage still matters, that conviction still wins, and that ordinary Americans can reclaim their future. I believe deeply in that mission. I 've lived it my entire life. And I'm grateful to have so many determined patriots standing alongside me as we work toward a safer, stronger, more united future. | | For Colorado and for America, Victor Marx P.S. If you've gotten this far, there's something I need you to know: You 're not just someone reading an email -- you're someone whose voice and values matter deeply in shaping the direction of this mission. , and your involvement strengthens the momentum we've already begun to build. -Victor | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- | | PAID FOR BY VICTOR MARX FOR GOVERNOR Registered Agent: Gregory Carlson | | P.O. Box 156 Florence, CO 81226

You’ve probably heard by now that I officially launched my reelection campaign last week.

And so far, I’ve heard from a lot of folks on the campaign trail about the issues that matter most to them and their families ahead of the midterms.

**And I gotta say, what I’m hearing is pretty straightforward:**
* _Folks are struggling to make ends meet due to rising prices and stagnant wages_

* _They’re tired of the unchecked extremism we’re seeing from this Trump administration that is making Americans less safe_

* _And they want leaders who put people first, not politics_

name: David Hogg
subject: [ENDORSEMENT] Nabeela Syed for Illinois State Senate.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
Nabeela Syed has a strong track record of getting the job done.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20251209-1&t=1&akid=2922%2E587540%2EAFfYYL)
I have some exciting news, Peter:
**I’m proud to announce that Leaders We Deserve is endorsing Nabeela Syed for the Illinois State Senate.**
[****](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20251209_1_nabeela.GqBDpe&refcode=em20251209-2922&amount=5&t=2&akid=2922%2E587540%2EAFfYYL)
Years ago when I was a Junior in college, long before we created Leaders We Deserve, I wanted to help elect more young progressives. One of the people I came across was a young woman running for a purple State House seat in Illinois. Her name was Nabeela. She was a young progressive who I thought would be a great example of how progressives can actually win in purple seats. I tweeted a bunch asking for people to support her and even hosted Twitter livestreams from my dorm room with Nabeela, soon-to-be Congressman Maxwell Frost, and other young people running for office. That year, Nabeela won by just about 3,000 votes.
She and her race were one of the original inspirations for creating Leaders We Deserve. So when she told me she was going to run for State Senate, supporting her was a no brainer. Not just because she is a great young candidate, but because of all she has accomplished while in the State House — **where she’s passed 21 bills into law, mainly focusing on issues like lowering costs for working families and protecting access to healthcare.**
**[If you’re ready to help elect Gen Z leaders who will step up and get the job done, will you split a $5 contribution to Nabeela’s campaign and Leaders We Deserve today?](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20251209_1_nabeela.GqBDpe&refcode=em20251209-2922&amount=5&t=3&akid=2922%2E587540%2EAFfYYL)**
Nabeela is running for Illinois State Senate in the 26th District to build on the work she’s already doing in Springfield and bring that same community-rooted approach to the Senate. She’s a lifelong resident of the district and has built her campaign around being present, showing up, listening, and translating what she hears from her constituents into policy.
Her work includes legislation that will cap prescription drug costs, expand breast cancer screening (legislation she worked on after learning of a constituent who got an incredibly late diagnosis due to insurance delays), secure paid leave for organ donors, and make voting more accessible to young people.
Nabeela has also prioritized improvements to school funding through Illinois’ Evidence-Based Funding approach. This work is aimed at strengthening public education while reducing dependence on local property taxes.
As a Gen Z lawmaker, she’s helped make it clear that sustained investment in public schools is foundational to economic stability, stronger communities, and long-term affordability.
But this race is going to be a competitive one, Peter — IL SD-26 is widely viewed as a top competitive seat going into 2026. The district’s most recent State Senate election in 2022 was decided by _only 385 votes._
Nabeela’s Republican opponent, Darby Hills, was just appointed in February 2025, and we know that her opponent and their GOP supporters are going to spend any amount they can to keep their seat in the State Senate.
**The outcome of this election could hinge on whether Nabeela’s campaign has the resources to run a full-scale voter-contact program, so this campaign will need the resources to support her work and deliver for Illinoisans.**
[**Peter, can we count on you to split $5 between Nabeela’s campaign and Leaders We Deserve right now to ensure her campaign has enough resources to win in 2026?**](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20251209_1_nabeela.GqBDpe&refcode=em20251209-2922&amount=5&t=4&akid=2922%2E587540%2EAFfYYL)
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20251209_1_nabeela.GqBDpe&amount=5&refcode=em20251209-2922&t=23&akid=2922%2E587540%2EAFfYYL)**
Thank you,
David Hogg
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-footer?refcode=em20251209-1&t=24&refcode2=2922_587540_AFfYYL&akid=2922%2E587540%2EAFfYYL)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=25&akid=2922%2E587540%2EAFfYYL)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=26&akid=2922%2E587540%2EAFfYYL)

To give by check:
Leaders We Deserve
611 Pennsylvania Ave SE
#192
Washington, DC 20003
Email us: [info@leaderswedeserve.com](mailto:info@leaderswedeserve.com)
PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE.COM, NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE

name: Mark Kelly
subject: I will not give up on our country
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bennet for Governor
body:
It has given me too much, and I’ll never be able to repay that debt. I still believe in our democracy, our values, and our Constitution.
| | |
---
Folks,
If you can believe it, things didn’t stop when Trump called for my hanging. It didn’t stop with the threat of a court-martial from Pete Hegseth, either. Because now, the FBI wants to talk to me and other members of Congress.
All for saying things the president didn’t like to hear.
If you’re feeling like this isn’t the country you grew up in, I don’t blame you. But I hope you aren’t giving up on it, folks.
I certainly haven’t. This country has given me too much, and I’ll never be able to repay that debt. I still believe in our democracy, our values, and our Constitution.
There’s no chance in hell that somebody like Donald Trump is going to stop me from serving the people of this nation. None.
If he thought all of this was going to shut me up, he’s got another thing coming.
And if you’d be willing to help support me as we gear up to fight back against the attacks coming our way from Donald Trump and his Administration, please:
**Split a donation between Michael Bennet and me. It would mean a lot. **
_If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately and be split between Mark Kelly and Michael Bennet:_ | | | [Donate $5](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120952047/587888744/1446759855?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=jtk1030-bu-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmZjEyOTc0YS00ZmQ1LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=plm1uQb7RwgRhaE8R6GrFvTRQCHHYMGgOkKI5MVCXHw=)

I certainly haven’t. This country has given me too much, and I’ll never be able to repay that debt. I still believe in our democracy, our values, and our Constitution. There’s no chance in hell that somebody like Donald Trump is going to stop me from serving the people of this nation. None. If he thought all of this was going to shut me up, he’s got another thing coming. And if you’d be willing to help support me as we gear up to fight back against the attacks coming our way from Donald Trump and his Administration, please: Split a donation between Michael Bennet and me. It would mean a lot. If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately and be split between Mark Kelly and Michael Bennet: | | | = | | = | | = | | = Thank you, Mark | Bennet for Governor PO Box 270117 Louisville, CO 80027 United States Paid for by Bennet for Governor. Registered Agent: Tracie Moore. By clicking the links above, you certify that you are responsible for paying all charges incurred in using the debit or credit card to be charged, and that your personal funds will be the true source of the contribution. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Click here to from future emails. |
