name: Powered by People
subject: Trump didn’t see this coming
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY POWERED BY PEOPLE
body:
[1]Powered By People

Texas has new gerrymandered maps thanks to Donald Trump, Greg Abbott, and
Republicans in the state legislature trying to steal five Democratic
seats.

Here’s what they didn’t see coming: we’re not throwing in the towel and
giving up. These new districts aren’t predetermined. They’re competitive.
They’re within reach. And Beto even won them while running statewide in
2018. But we can’t just hope it happens again—we have to do the work to
make it happen.

At Powered by People, we don’t wait for change. We organize for it. We
have boots on the ground, registering new voters, recruiting volunteers,
hosting community events, and making sure everyone has the information
they need to show up and vote. Every person we reach, every neighbor we
connect with, every vote we turn out brings us closer to winning these new
districts for the people.

But this work takes resources in a state as big as Texas. To secure real,
lasting power in 2026 and beyond, we need to scale up our organizing
program now.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/57169?c0=pbp-em-20251209-2.lu1BcP&refcode=ema20251209-240522&t=1002&akid=240522%2E444211%2EdBTtYw ]Will you pitch in $5—one dollar for each of the districts Republicans
are trying to steal—to power this organizing and voter registration?

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/57169?c0=pbp-em-20251209-2.lu1BcP&refcode=ema20251209-240522&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1003&akid=240522%2E444211%2EdBTtYw ]DONATE $5

And if you can give a little more, [ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/57171?c0=pbp-em-20251209-2.ExEQxr&refcode=ema20251209-240522&t=1004&akid=240522%2E444211%2EdBTtYw ]$50—ten dollars per district—goes
even further toward building the on-the-ground infrastructure we need to
win.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/57171?c0=pbp-em-20251209-2.ExEQxr&refcode=ema20251209-240522&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1005&akid=240522%2E444211%2EdBTtYw ]DONATE $50

When we organize together, we win together. And right now, we have the
chance to build something that lasts.

Thank you,

Powered by People

name: Pramila Jayapal
subject: This is why I’m fighting so hard for Medicare For All
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
Last week, my campaign sent out a survey asking how rising health care costs are affecting you and your families.
# [](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/892?refcode=em20251209-5695-footer&t=1&akid=5695%2E420207%2E5DkFNY)
Peter,
Last week, my campaign sent out a survey asking how rising health care costs are affecting you and your families. With key subsidies in the Affordable Care Act expiring, costs are rising for millions — and it was important to me to hear directly from the people impacted about what that will mean.
**In total, over 72% of the people who responded to the survey confirmed that their costs have gone up this year, and many said their costs will increase even more in the new year.**
In response to these cost increases, some families are downgrading their coverage, others are cutting back in other areas, and some are dropping their insurance altogether. Here are just a few of the stories people shared:
* _“Our premiums will jump from $565 to over $2,000 per month. We will stay insured, but it’s eating into our retirement savings.”

_
* _“It puts a strain on the everyday necessities.”

_
* _“We have to reduce heat in several rooms and use more blankets.”

_
* _“I can’t afford my health care costs despite having insurance, and have to put off necessary medications, procedures and visits.”_

Peter, this is the reality that millions of people across this country are facing. While insurance and pharmaceutical companies are making higher profits than ever, working people are skipping doctor’s visits, cutting back on their grocery budgets, and eating into their savings just to pay for the care they need.
In the wealthiest country in the world, this should not be happening. That’s why I’m fighting so hard to make Medicare For All a reality. But I can’t do it alone — it’s going to take a nationwide movement to take on and defeat the corporate interests who are propping up and profiting off of the current system.
**[So Peter, if you’re in a position to donate, will you pitch in $3 today to help me keep fighting to end this cruel system and finally guarantee health care as a human right for everyone in the country?](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-email-20251209.T35naY&refcode=em20251209-5695&amount=3&t=2&akid=5695%2E420207%2E5DkFNY)**
**[Contribute $3](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-email-20251209.T35naY&amount=3&refcode=em20251209-5695&t=21&akid=5695%2E420207%2E5DkFNY)**
In solidarity,
Pramila Jayapal
**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-footer?refcode=em20251209-5695-footer&t=22&refcode2=5695_420207_5DkFNY&akid=5695%2E420207%2E5DkFNY)**
---
[](https://www.facebook.com/pramila.jayapal) | [](https://twitter.com/pramilajayapal) | [](https://www.instagram.com/pramilajayapal/)
Email us: info@pramilaforcongress.com
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, and small-dollar contributions sustain our movement for transformative change. To contribute by check, please address to Pramila for Congress, PO Box 21912, Seattle, WA 98111.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/did-your-info-change/?t=23&akid=5695%2E420207%2E5DkFNY)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=24&akid=5695%2E420207%2E5DkFNY)

Paid for by Pramila for Congress

name: James Talarico
subject: This race just got more expensive.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Talarico for Texas
body:
| | | | | | | | | | |
---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---
Hey Peter, it’s James Talarico with an update.

We are in a new race. And in the last 24 hours, this campaign just got a whole lot more expensive. (As if it wasn’t already ridiculously expensive.)

So I cannot overstate how important this moment is. The strength of our movement as the field grows sets the tone for this new race. If we demonstrate our strength, our momentum builds on itself. More people step up and join our movement to win back power for working people in a system rigged by billionaire mega-donors.

But National Republicans have made clear they’ll do anything it takes to make sure I’m not the Democratic nominee. They don’t want me as their opponent.

[**So I am asking you to have my back right now by making a contribution. We need all the resources we can muster to get to work and win this thing. I would not ask if this wasn’t a critical moment for our movement.**](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-launch.bF5RK3&refcode=em251209-2-ak&t=1&akid=305%2E1347274%2Ex7Y_t7) James Talarico |

name: JusticeDemocrats.com
subject: Melat will stand up to bullies like AIPAC
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

When Melat Kiros saw the horrific genocide unfolding in Gaza — and the
persecution of pro-Palestinian student protesters here in the United
States — she used her voice to speak out.

In 2023, she posted a response on Medium to an open letter signed by more
than 200 law firms urging law school deans to crack down on pro-Palestine
protests on their campuses. As a 2022 graduate of Notre Dame Law and an
associate at a law firm, Melat criticized the crackdown on college
students’ freedom of speech while condemning antisemitism.

As a result, she lost her job — ironically becoming the next young person
to be punished for speaking out against genocide and attacks on free
speech.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251209-mk.E_VFZU&refcode=em20251209-37264&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1002&akid=37264%2E1203867%2E3kpanC ]A screenshot of a headline reading 'FREE SPEECH: Firing of Law Firm
Associate for Anti-Israel Letter'

But Melat doesn’t regret using her voice to speak truth to power. As a
candidate for Congress in Colorado’s 1st district, she has the courage of
her convictions and will stand up to bullies like AIPAC, even if it costs
her her job.

On the other hand, there’s CO-01 incumbent Rep. Diana DeGette, who has
sold out to Big Pharma, corporate PACs, and fossil fuel lobbies and lacks
the courage to stand up for what’s right.

We can unseat a spineless, corporate-backed Democrat from Congress and
replace her with a progressive, working-class champion with moral clarity
and bravery next year, but only with your grassroots support.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251209-mk.E_VFZU&refcode=em20251209-37264&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1003&akid=37264%2E1203867%2E3kpanC ]Make a split contribution of $5 or more to Justice
Democrats and Melat’s campaign to help her win this primary election just
six months from now!

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251209-mk.E_VFZU&refcode=em20251209-37264&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1022&akid=37264%2E1203867%2E3kpanC ]Contribute $5

clean_body: [1]Justice Democrats Peter, When Melat Kiros saw the horrific genocide unfolding in Gaza — and the persecution of pro-Palestinian student protesters here in the United States — she used her voice to speak out. In 2023, she posted a response on Medium to an open letter signed by more than 200 law firms urging law school deans to crack down on pro-Palestine protests on their campuses. As a 2022 graduate of Notre Dame Law and an associate at a law firm, Melat criticized the crackdown on college students’ freedom of speech while condemning antisemitism. As a result, she lost her job — ironically becoming the next young person to be punished for speaking out against genocide and attacks on free speech. [ ]A screenshot of a headline reading 'FREE SPEECH: Firing of Law Firm Associate for Anti-Israel Letter' But Melat doesn’t regret using her voice to speak truth to power. As a candidate for Congress in Colorado’s 1st district, she has the courage of her convictions and will stand up to bullies like AIPAC, even if it costs her her job. On the other hand, there’s CO-01 incumbent Rep. Diana DeGette, who has sold out to Big Pharma, corporate PACs, and fossil fuel lobbies and lacks the courage to stand up for what’s right. We can unseat a spineless, corporate-backed Democrat from Congress and replace her with a progressive, working-class champion with moral clarity and bravery next year, but only with your grassroots support. [ ]Make a split contribution of $5 or more to Justice Democrats and Melat’s campaign to help her win this primary election just six months from now!
